authors_text: Aryaman Jeendgar,Tanmay Devale,Soma S Dhavala,Snehanshu Saha
title: LogGENE: A smooth alternative to check loss for Deep Healthcare Inference Tasks
title_zh: LogGENE：检查深度医疗推理任务损失的平滑替代方案
abstract: Mining large datasets and obtaining calibrated predictions from tem is of immediate relevance and utility in reliable deep learning. In our work, we develop methods for Deep neural networks based inferences in such datasets like the Gene Expression. However, unlike typical Deep learning methods, our inferential technique, while achieving state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy, can also provide explanations, and report uncertainty estimates. We adopt the Quantile Regression framework to predict full conditional quantiles for a given set of housekeeping gene expressions. Conditional quantiles, in addition to being useful in providing rich interpretations of the predictions, are also robust to measurement noise. Our technique is particularly consequential in High-throughput Genomics, an area which is ushering a new era in personalized health care, and targeted drug design and delivery. However, check loss, used in quantile regression to drive the estimation process is not differentiable. We propose log-cosh as a smooth-alternative to the check loss. We apply our methods on GEO microarray dataset. We also extend the method to binary classification setting. Furthermore, we investigate other consequences of the smoothness of the loss in faster convergence. We further apply the classification framework to other healthcare inference tasks such as heart disease, breast cancer, diabetes etc. As a test of generalization ability of our framework, other non-healthcare related data sets for regression and classification tasks are also evaluated.
summary: Mining large datasets and obtaining calibrated predictions from tem is of immediate relevance and utility in reliable deep learning. In our work, we develop methods for Deep neural networks based inferences in such datasets like the Gene Expression. However, unlike typical Deep learning methods, our inferential technique, while achieving state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy, can also provide explanations, and report uncertainty estimates. We adopt the Quantile Regression framework to predict full conditional quantiles for a given set of housekeeping gene expressions. Conditional quantiles, in addition to being useful in providing rich interpretations of the predictions, are also robust to measurement noise. Our technique is particularly consequential in High-throughput Genomics, an area which is ushering a new era in personalized health care, and targeted drug design and delivery. However, check loss, used in quantile regression to drive the estimation process is not differentiable. We propose log-cosh as a smooth-alternative to the check loss. We apply our methods on GEO microarray dataset. We also extend the method to binary classification setting. Furthermore, we investigate other consequences of the smoothness of the loss in faster convergence. We further apply the classification framework to other healthcare inference tasks such as heart disease, breast cancer, diabetes etc. As a test of generalization ability of our framework, other non-healthcare related data sets for regression and classification tasks are also evaluated.
abstract_zh: 挖掘大型数据集并从 tem 获得校准预测对于可靠的深度学习具有直接的相关性和实用性。在我们的工作中，我们开发了基于深度神经网络在基因表达等数据集中进行推理的方法。然而，与典型的深度学习方法不同，我们的推理技术在准确性方面实现了最先进的性能的同时，还可以提供解释并报告不确定性估计。我们采用分位数回归框架来预测给定的一组管家基因表达的完整条件分位数。条件分位数除了可用于提供丰富的预测解释之外，对于测量噪声也具有鲁棒性。我们的技术在高通量基因组学领域尤其重要，该领域正在开创个性化医疗保健以及靶向药物设计和递送的新时代。然而，在分位数回归中用于驱动估计过程的检查损失是不可微分的。我们建议使用 log-cosh 作为检查损失的平滑替代方案。我们将我们的方法应用于 GEO 微阵列数据集。我们还将该方法扩展到二元分类设置。此外，我们还研究了更快收敛中损失的平滑性的其他后果。我们进一步将分类框架应用于其他医疗保健推理任务，例如心脏病、乳腺癌、糖尿病等。作为对我们框架的泛化能力的测试，还评估了用于回归和分类任务的其他非医疗保健相关数据集。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LogGENE方法，通过平滑的log-cosh损失函数替代传统分位数回归中的非可微检查损失，用于深度医疗推理任务，以提高预测的可解释性和鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 医疗领域需要可靠且可解释的深度学习模型，传统检查损失函数因不可微性限制了模型优化，且需处理高通量基因组数据中的测量噪声问题。
AI.method: 基于分位数回归框架，采用log-cosh损失函数替代检查损失，预测管家基因表达的条件分位数，并扩展至二分类任务，应用于医疗数据集（如心脏病、乳腺癌）及非医疗数据集。
AI.result: 在GEO微阵列数据集及多种医疗任务中达到最先进精度，提供不确定性估计与解释，平滑损失函数加速收敛，且在非医疗数据集上验证了泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: LogGENE通过平滑损失函数提升了医疗推理的鲁棒性与可解释性，为个性化医疗和药物设计提供新方法，并适用于更广泛的回归与分类任务。

We apply our methods on GEO microarray dataset. We also extend the method to binary classification setting. We further apply the classification framework to other healthcare inference tasks such as heart disease, breast cancer, diabetes etc. As a test of generalization ability of our framework, other non-healthcare related data sets for regression and classification tasks are also evaluated. 挖掘大型数据集并从 tem 获得校准预测对于可靠的深度学习具有直接的相关性和实用性。在我们的工作中，我们开发了基于深度神经网络在基因表达等数据集中进行推理的方法。然而，与典型的深度学习方法不同，我们的推理技术在准确性方面实现了最先进的性能的同时，还可以提供解释并报告不确定性估计。我们采用分位数回归框架来预测给定的一组管家基因表达的完整条件分位数。条件分位数除了可用于提供丰富的预测解释之外，对于测量噪声也具有鲁棒性。我们的技术在高通量基因组学领域尤其重要，该领域正在开创个性化医疗保健以及靶向药物设计和递送的新时代。然而，在分位数回归中用于驱动估计过程的检查损失是不可微分的。我们建议使用 log-cosh 作为检查损失的平滑替代方案。我们将我们的方法应用于 GEO 微阵列数据集。我们还将该方法扩展到二元分类设置。此外，我们还研究了更快收敛中损失的平滑性的其他后果。我们进一步将分类框架应用于其他医疗保健推理任务，例如心脏病、乳腺癌、糖尿病等。作为对我们框架的泛化能力的测试，还评估了用于回归和分类任务的其他非医疗保健相关数据集。 本文提出LogGENE方法，通过平滑的log-cosh损失函数替代传统分位数回归中的非可微检查损失，用于深度医疗推理任务，以提高预测的可解释性和鲁棒性。 Aryaman Jeendgar,Tanmay Devale,Soma S Dhavala,Snehanshu Saha cs.LG,cs.NE,stat.ML

authors_text: Jiangning Zhang,Xiangtai Li,Yabiao Wang,Chengjie Wang,Yibo Yang,Yong Liu,Dacheng Tao
title: EATFormer: Improving Vision Transformer Inspired by Evolutionary Algorithm
title_zh: EATFormer：受进化算法启发改进视觉 Transformer
abstract: Motivated by biological evolution, this paper explains the rationality of Vision Transformer by analogy with the proven practical evolutionary algorithm (EA) and derives that both have consistent mathematical formulation. Then inspired by effective EA variants, we propose a novel pyramid EATFormer backbone that only contains the proposed EA-based transformer (EAT) block, which consists of three residual parts, i.e., Multi-scale region aggregation, global and local interaction, and feed-forward network modules, to model multi-scale, interactive, and individual information separately. Moreover, we design a task-related head docked with transformer backbone to complete final information fusion more flexibly and improve a modulated deformable MSA to dynamically model irregular locations. Massive quantitative and quantitative experiments on image classification, downstream tasks, and explanatory experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of our approach over state-of-the-art methods. E.g., our Mobile (1.8 M), Tiny (6.1 M), Small (24.3 M), and Base (49.0 M) models achieve 69.4, 78.4, 83.1, and 83.9 Top-1 only trained on ImageNet-1K with naive training recipe; EATFormer-Tiny/Small/Base armed Mask-R-CNN obtain 45.4/47.4/49.0 box AP and 41.4/42.9/44.2 mask AP on COCO detection, surpassing contemporary MPViT-T, Swin-T, and Swin-S by 0.6/1.4/0.5 box AP and 0.4/1.3/0.9 mask AP separately with less FLOPs; Our EATFormer-Small/Base achieve 47.3/49.3 mIoU on ADE20K by Upernet that exceeds Swin-T/S by 2.8/1.7. Code is available at https://github.com/zhangzjn/EATFormer.
summary: Motivated by biological evolution, this paper explains the rationality of Vision Transformer by analogy with the proven practical evolutionary algorithm (EA) and derives that both have consistent mathematical formulation. Then inspired by effective EA variants, we propose a novel pyramid EATFormer backbone that only contains the proposed EA-based transformer (EAT) block, which consists of three residual parts, i.e., Multi-scale region aggregation, global and local interaction, and feed-forward network modules, to model multi-scale, interactive, and individual information separately. Moreover, we design a task-related head docked with transformer backbone to complete final information fusion more flexibly and improve a modulated deformable MSA to dynamically model irregular locations. Massive quantitative and quantitative experiments on image classification, downstream tasks, and explanatory experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of our approach over state-of-the-art methods. E.g., our Mobile (1.8 M), Tiny (6.1 M), Small (24.3 M), and Base (49.0 M) models achieve 69.4, 78.4, 83.1, and 83.9 Top-1 only trained on ImageNet-1K with naive training recipe; EATFormer-Tiny/Small/Base armed Mask-R-CNN obtain 45.4/47.4/49.0 box AP and 41.4/42.9/44.2 mask AP on COCO detection, surpassing contemporary MPViT-T, Swin-T, and Swin-S by 0.6/1.4/0.5 box AP and 0.4/1.3/0.9 mask AP separately with less FLOPs; Our EATFormer-Small/Base achieve 47.3/49.3 mIoU on ADE20K by Upernet that exceeds Swin-T/S by 2.8/1.7. Code is available at https://github.com/zhangzjn/EATFormer.
abstract_zh: 本文以生物进化为动力，类比经过验证的实用进化算法（EA）来解释Vision Transformer的合理性，并推导出两者具有一致的数学公式。然后，受有效 EA 变体的启发，我们提出了一种新颖的金字塔 EATFormer 主干，其中仅包含所提出的基于 EA 的变压器（EAT）块，该块由三个剩余部分组成，即多尺度区域聚合、全局和局部交互以及前馈网络模块，以分别对多尺度、交互和个体信息进行建模。此外，我们设计了一个与变压器主干对接的任务相关头，以更灵活地完成最终信息融合，并改进调制可变形MSA以动态模拟不规则位置。关于图像分类、下游任务和解释性实验的大量定量和定量实验证明了我们的方法相对于最先进方法的有效性和优越性。例如，我们的 Mobile (1.8 M)、Tiny (6.1 M)、Small (24.3 M) 和 Base (49.0 M) 模型仅在 ImageNet-1K 上使用朴素训练配方进行训练，即可达到 69.4、78.4、83.1 和 83.9 Top-1； EATFormer-Tiny/Small/Base Armed Mask-R-CNN 在 COCO 检测上获得 45.4/47.4/49.0 box AP 和 41.4/42.9/44.2 mask AP，以更少的 FLOPs 分别超越当代 MPViT-T、Swin-T 和 Swin-S 0.6/1.4/0.5 box AP 和 0.4/1.3/0.9 mask AP；我们的 EATFormer-Small/Base 在 ADE20K 上通过 Upernet 达到 47.3/49.3 mIoU，比 Swin-T/S 高出 2.8/1.7。代码可在 https://github.com/zhangzjn/EATFormer 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出受进化算法启发的EATFormer视觉Transformer模型，通过类比进化算法的数学公式改进传统Transformer结构。
AI.motivation: 受生物进化启发，发现进化算法与Vision Transformer在数学形式上具有一致性，旨在通过有效进化算法变体优化Transformer架构。
AI.method: 设计包含多尺度区域聚合、全局局部交互和前馈网络的EA-based Transformer模块，结合任务相关头和动态模态可变形MSA实现多尺度信息建模。
AI.result: 在ImageNet-1K上以1.8M参数达到69.4 Top-1准确率，COCO目标检测中EATFormer-Tiny/Small/Base分别超越Swin-T/S 0.6/1.4/0.5 box AP，ADE20K语义分割达到47.3/49.3 mIoU。
AI.conclusion: EATFormer通过进化算法指导的模块设计，在保持低计算量的同时实现了优于现有SOTA方法的性能，验证了生物进化启发的有效性。

authors_text: Jing Zhang
title: Knowledge Learning with Crowdsourcing: A Brief Review and Systematic Perspective
title_zh: 众包知识学习：简要回顾与系统视角
abstract: Big data have the characteristics of enormous volume, high velocity, diversity, value-sparsity, and uncertainty, which lead the knowledge learning from them full of challenges. With the emergence of crowdsourcing, versatile information can be obtained on-demand so that the wisdom of crowds is easily involved to facilitate the knowledge learning process. During the past thirteen years, researchers in the AI community made great efforts to remove the obstacles in the field of learning from crowds. This concentrated survey paper comprehensively reviews the technical progress in crowdsourcing learning from a systematic perspective that includes three dimensions of data, models, and learning processes. In addition to reviewing existing important work, the paper places a particular emphasis on providing some promising blueprints on each dimension as well as discussing the lessons learned from our past research work, which will light up the way for new researchers and encourage them to pursue new contributions.
summary: Big data have the characteristics of enormous volume, high velocity, diversity, value-sparsity, and uncertainty, which lead the knowledge learning from them full of challenges. With the emergence of crowdsourcing, versatile information can be obtained on-demand so that the wisdom of crowds is easily involved to facilitate the knowledge learning process. During the past thirteen years, researchers in the AI community made great efforts to remove the obstacles in the field of learning from crowds. This concentrated survey paper comprehensively reviews the technical progress in crowdsourcing learning from a systematic perspective that includes three dimensions of data, models, and learning processes. In addition to reviewing existing important work, the paper places a particular emphasis on providing some promising blueprints on each dimension as well as discussing the lessons learned from our past research work, which will light up the way for new researchers and encourage them to pursue new contributions.
abstract_zh: 大数据具有海量、高速度、多样性、价值稀疏性和不确定性等特点，使得大数据的知识学习充满挑战。众包的出现，可以按需获取多样化的信息，从而轻松参与群体的智慧，促进知识的学习过程。在过去的十三年里，人工智能界的研究人员为消除群体学习领域的障碍做出了巨大的努力。这篇集中调查论文从数据、模型和学习过程三个维度系统地回顾了众包学习的技术进展。除了回顾现有的重要工作外，本文还特别强调在每个维度上提供一些有前景的蓝图，并讨论从我们过去的研究工作中汲取的经验教训，这将为新的研究人员指明道路，并鼓励他们做出新的贡献。

AI.tldr: 本文系统回顾了过去十三年众包知识学习的技术进展，并从数据、模型和学习过程三个维度提出未来研究方向。
AI.motivation: 大数据时代知识学习面临数据稀疏性、不确定性等挑战，而众包技术能提供多样化信息辅助知识学习，亟需系统性总结与展望。
AI.method: 通过系统性综述方法，从数据、模型和学习流程三个维度分析现有研究，提炼关键进展并提出各维度的未来研究蓝图。
AI.result: 全面梳理了众包知识学习领域的重要成果，总结了技术演进规律，并为后续研究提供了可遵循的技术路线与经验教训。
AI.conclusion: 众包知识学习需进一步突破数据质量控制、模型可解释性及动态学习机制等瓶颈，未来研究应注重跨学科方法的融合应用。

authors_text: Jongseong Chae,Seungyul Han,Whiyoung Jung,Myungsik Cho,Sungho Choi,Youngchul Sung
title: Robust Imitation Learning against Variations in Environment Dynamics
title_zh: 针对环境动态变化的鲁棒模仿学习
abstract: In this paper, we propose a robust imitation learning (IL) framework that improves the robustness of IL when environment dynamics are perturbed. The existing IL framework trained in a single environment can catastrophically fail with perturbations in environment dynamics because it does not capture the situation that underlying environment dynamics can be changed. Our framework effectively deals with environments with varying dynamics by imitating multiple experts in sampled environment dynamics to enhance the robustness in general variations in environment dynamics. In order to robustly imitate the multiple sample experts, we minimize the risk with respect to the Jensen-Shannon divergence between the agent's policy and each of the sample experts. Numerical results show that our algorithm significantly improves robustness against dynamics perturbations compared to conventional IL baselines.
summary: In this paper, we propose a robust imitation learning (IL) framework that improves the robustness of IL when environment dynamics are perturbed. The existing IL framework trained in a single environment can catastrophically fail with perturbations in environment dynamics because it does not capture the situation that underlying environment dynamics can be changed. Our framework effectively deals with environments with varying dynamics by imitating multiple experts in sampled environment dynamics to enhance the robustness in general variations in environment dynamics. In order to robustly imitate the multiple sample experts, we minimize the risk with respect to the Jensen-Shannon divergence between the agent's policy and each of the sample experts. Numerical results show that our algorithm significantly improves robustness against dynamics perturbations compared to conventional IL baselines.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种鲁棒的模仿学习（IL）框架，该框架可以在环境动态受到扰动时提高 IL 的鲁棒性。在单一环境中训练的现有 IL 框架可能会因环境动态的扰动而发生灾难性的失败，因为它无法捕获底层环境动态可以改变的情况。我们的框架通过模仿采样环境动态中的多个专家来有效地处理具有不同动态的环境，以增强环境动态的一般变化的鲁棒性。为了稳健地模仿多个样本专家，我们最小化了代理策略与每个样本专家之间的 Jensen-Shannon 分歧的风险。数值结果表明，与传统的 IL 基线相比，我们的算法显着提高了针对动态扰动的鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种鲁棒模仿学习框架，以提高环境动态变化下的算法鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有模仿学习框架在单一环境中训练，无法应对环境动态变化导致的灾难性失效。
AI.method: 通过模仿多专家策略并最小化智能体策略与专家间的Jensen-Shannon散度风险。
AI.result: 算法在动态扰动环境下显著优于传统模仿学习基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效提升了对抗环境动态变化的鲁棒性，验证了多专家模仿策略的有效性。

authors_text: Junyu Liu,Zexi Lin,Liang Jiang
title: Laziness, Barren Plateau, and Noise in Machine Learning
title_zh: 机器学习中的懒惰、贫瘠高原和噪音
abstract: We define \emph{laziness} to describe a large suppression of variational parameter updates for neural networks, classical or quantum. In the quantum case, the suppression is exponential in the number of qubits for randomized variational quantum circuits. We discuss the difference between laziness and \emph{barren plateau} in quantum machine learning created by quantum physicists in \cite{mcclean2018barren} for the flatness of the loss function landscape during gradient descent. We address a novel theoretical understanding of those two phenomena in light of the theory of neural tangent kernels. For noiseless quantum circuits, without the measurement noise, the loss function landscape is complicated in the overparametrized regime with a large number of trainable variational angles. Instead, around a random starting point in optimization, there are large numbers of local minima that are good enough and could minimize the mean square loss function, where we still have quantum laziness, but we do not have barren plateaus. However, the complicated landscape is not visible within a limited number of iterations, and low precision in quantum control and quantum sensing. Moreover, we look at the effect of noises during optimization by assuming intuitive noise models, and show that variational quantum algorithms are noise-resilient in the overparametrization regime. Our work precisely reformulates the quantum barren plateau statement towards a precision statement and justifies the statement in certain noise models, injects new hope toward near-term variational quantum algorithms, and provides theoretical connections toward classical machine learning. Our paper provides conceptual perspectives about quantum barren plateaus, together with discussions about the gradient descent dynamics in \cite{together}.

In the quantum case, the suppression is exponential in the number of qubits for randomized variational quantum circuits. We discuss the difference between laziness and \emph{barren plateau} in quantum machine learning created by quantum physicists in \cite{mcclean2018barren} for the flatness of the loss function landscape during gradient descent. We address a novel theoretical understanding of those two phenomena in light of the theory of neural tangent kernels. For noiseless quantum circuits, without the measurement noise, the loss function landscape is complicated in the overparametrized regime with a large number of trainable variational angles. Instead, around a random starting point in optimization, there are large numbers of local minima that are good enough and could minimize the mean square loss function, where we still have quantum laziness, but we do not have barren plateaus. However, the complicated landscape is not visible within a limited number of iterations, and low precision in quantum control and quantum sensing. Moreover, we look at the effect of noises during optimization by assuming intuitive noise models, and show that variational quantum algorithms are noise-resilient in the overparametrization regime. Our work precisely reformulates the quantum barren plateau statement towards a precision statement and justifies the statement in certain noise models, injects new hope toward near-term variational quantum algorithms, and provides theoretical connections toward classical machine learning. Our paper provides conceptual perspectives about quantum barren plateaus, together with discussions about the gradient descent dynamics in \cite{together}.
abstract_zh: 我们定义\emph{惰性}来描述对经典或量子神经网络的变分参数更新的大量抑制。在量子情况下，随机变分量子电路的量子位数量的抑制是指数级的。我们讨论了量子物理学家在 \cite{mcclean2018barren} 中创建的量子机器学习中的惰性和 \emph{barrenplateau} 之间的区别，以求梯度下降过程中损失函数景观的平坦度。我们根据神经切线核理论对这两种现象提出了一种新颖的理论理解。对于无噪声量子电路，在没有测量噪声的情况下，在具有大量可训练变分角度的过参数化区域中，损失函数景观是复杂的。相反，围绕优化中的随机起点，存在大量足够好的局部最小值，并且可以最小化均方损失函数，其中我们仍然具有量子惰性，但我们没有贫瘠的高原。然而，复杂的景观在有限的迭代次数内是不可见的，并且量子控制和量子传感的精度较低。此外，我们通过假设直观的噪声模型来研究优化过程中噪声的影响，并表明变分量子算法在超参数化状态下具有抗噪声能力。我们的工作精确地将量子贫瘠高原陈述重新表述为精确陈述，并在某些噪声模型中证明了该陈述的合理性，为近期变分量子算法注入了新的希望，并为经典机器学习提供了理论联系。我们的论文提供了有关量子贫瘠高原的概念观点，以及有关 \cite{together} 中梯度下降动力学的讨论。

AI.tldr: 本文研究量子与经典机器学习中的'懒惰'现象、 barren plateau（损失函数平坦性）及噪声影响，通过神经切线核理论分析二者区别，并探讨变分量子算法在噪声环境下的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 解决量子机器学习中梯度消失（barren plateau）问题，明确'懒惰'与barren plateau的理论差异，为近期内变分量子算法的可行性提供理论支持。
AI.method: 基于神经切线核理论区分laziness与barren plateau，结合噪声模型分析过度参数化下变分量子算法的优化动态，通过理论推导重新表述barren plateau的精度条件。
AI.result: 发现无噪声时过度参数化导致复杂损失景观但无barren plateau，噪声环境下算法具备鲁棒性；重新定义barren plateau的精度依赖性，建立与经典机器学习的理论关联。
AI.conclusion: 量子barren plateau本质是精度问题，过度参数化与噪声可缓解其影响，为近期变分量子算法应用注入新希望，并揭示量子与经典机器学习的理论共性。

authors_text: Ke Zou,Xuedong Yuan,Xiaojing Shen,Meng Wang,Huazhu Fu
title: TBraTS: Trusted Brain Tumor Segmentation
title_zh: TBraTS：可信脑肿瘤分割
abstract: Despite recent improvements in the accuracy of brain tumor segmentation, the results still exhibit low levels of confidence and robustness. Uncertainty estimation is one effective way to change this situation, as it provides a measure of confidence in the segmentation results. In this paper, we propose a trusted brain tumor segmentation network which can generate robust segmentation results and reliable uncertainty estimations without excessive computational burden and modification of the backbone network. In our method, uncertainty is modeled explicitly using subjective logic theory, which treats the predictions of backbone neural network as subjective opinions by parameterizing the class probabilities of the segmentation as a Dirichlet distribution. Meanwhile, the trusted segmentation framework learns the function that gathers reliable evidence from the feature leading to the final segmentation results. Overall, our unified trusted segmentation framework endows the model with reliability and robustness to out-of-distribution samples. To evaluate the effectiveness of our model in robustness and reliability, qualitative and quantitative experiments are conducted on the BraTS 2019 dataset.
summary: Despite recent improvements in the accuracy of brain tumor segmentation, the results still exhibit low levels of confidence and robustness. Uncertainty estimation is one effective way to change this situation, as it provides a measure of confidence in the segmentation results. In this paper, we propose a trusted brain tumor segmentation network which can generate robust segmentation results and reliable uncertainty estimations without excessive computational burden and modification of the backbone network. In our method, uncertainty is modeled explicitly using subjective logic theory, which treats the predictions of backbone neural network as subjective opinions by parameterizing the class probabilities of the segmentation as a Dirichlet distribution. Meanwhile, the trusted segmentation framework learns the function that gathers reliable evidence from the feature leading to the final segmentation results. Overall, our unified trusted segmentation framework endows the model with reliability and robustness to out-of-distribution samples. To evaluate the effectiveness of our model in robustness and reliability, qualitative and quantitative experiments are conducted on the BraTS 2019 dataset.
abstract_zh: 尽管最近脑肿瘤分割的准确性有所提高，但结果仍然表现出较低的置信度和鲁棒性。不确定性估计是改变这种情况的一种有效方法，因为它提供了分割结果的置信度。在本文中，我们提出了一种可信的脑肿瘤分割网络，它可以生成稳健的分割结果和可靠的不确定性估计，而无需过多的计算负担和骨干网络的修改。在我们的方法中，不确定性是使用主观逻辑理论明确建模的，该理论通过将分割的类概率参数化为狄利克雷分布，将骨干神经网络的预测视为主观意见。同时，可信分割框架学习从导致最终分割结果的特征中收集可靠证据的功能。总体而言，我们统一的可信分割框架赋予模型对分布外样本的可靠性和鲁棒性。为了评估我们的模型在稳健性和可靠性方面的有效性，在 BraTS 2019 数据集上进行了定性和定量实验。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可信的脑肿瘤分割网络TBraTS，通过不确定性估计提升分割结果的鲁棒性和可靠性。
AI.motivation: 现有脑肿瘤分割方法存在置信度低和鲁棒性不足的问题，需通过不确定性估计改善模型可靠性。
AI.method: 基于主观逻辑理论，将主干网络预测参数化为狄利克雷分布建模不确定性，同时设计证据收集函数整合特征信息生成最终分割结果。
AI.result: 在BraTS 2019数据集上通过定性和定量实验验证了模型在鲁棒性和可靠性方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该统一框架通过不确定性建模显著提升了模型对分布外样本的可靠性和分割鲁棒性。

search_text: TBraTS: Trusted Brain Tumor Segmentation TBraTS：可信脑肿瘤分割 Despite recent improvements in the accuracy of brain tumor segmentation, the results still exhibit low levels of confidence and robustness. Uncertainty estimation is one effective way to change this situation, as it provides a measure of confidence in the segmentation results. In this paper, we propose a trusted brain tumor segmentation network which can generate robust segmentation results and reliable uncertainty estimations without excessive computational burden and modification of the backbone network. In our method, uncertainty is modeled explicitly using subjective logic theory, which treats the predictions of backbone neural network as subjective opinions by parameterizing the class probabilities of the segmentation as a Dirichlet distribution. Meanwhile, the trusted segmentation framework learns the function that gathers reliable evidence from the feature leading to the final segmentation results. Overall, our unified trusted segmentation framework endows the model with reliability and robustness to out-of-distribution samples. To evaluate the effectiveness of our model in robustness and reliability, qualitative and quantitative experiments are conducted on the BraTS 2019 dataset. 尽管最近脑肿瘤分割的准确性有所提高，但结果仍然表现出较低的置信度和鲁棒性。不确定性估计是改变这种情况的一种有效方法，因为它提供了分割结果的置信度。在本文中，我们提出了一种可信的脑肿瘤分割网络，它可以生成稳健的分割结果和可靠的不确定性估计，而无需过多的计算负担和骨干网络的修改。在我们的方法中，不确定性是使用主观逻辑理论明确建模的，该理论通过将分割的类概率参数化为狄利克雷分布，将骨干神经网络的预测视为主观意见。同时，可信分割框架学习从导致最终分割结果的特征中收集可靠证据的功能。总体而言，我们统一的可信分割框架赋予模型对分布外样本的可靠性和鲁棒性。为了评估我们的模型在稳健性和可靠性方面的有效性，在 BraTS 2019 数据集上进行了定性和定量实验。 本文提出一种可信的脑肿瘤分割网络TBraTS，通过不确定性估计提升分割结果的鲁棒性和可靠性。 Ke Zou,Xuedong Yuan,Xiaojing Shen,Meng Wang,Huazhu Fu eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Wageesha Manamperi,Prasanga N. Samarasinghe,Thushara D. Abhayapala,Jihui Zhang
title: GMM based multi-stage Wiener filtering for low SNR speech enhancement
title_zh: 基于 GMM 的多级维纳滤波，用于低 SNR 语音增强
comment: 5 pages, 3 figures, submitted to a conference
comments: 5 pages, 3 figures, submitted to a conference
abstract: This paper proposes a single-channel speech enhancement method to reduce the noise and enhance speech at low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) levels and non-stationary noise conditions. Specifically, we focus on modeling the noise using a Gaussian mixture model (GMM) based on a multi-stage process with a parametric Wiener filter. The proposed noise model estimates a more accurate noise power spectral density (PSD), and allows for better generalization under various noise conditions compared to traditional Wiener filtering methods. Simulations show that the proposed approach can achieve better performance in terms of speech quality (PESQ) and intelligibility (STOI) at low SNR levels.
summary: This paper proposes a single-channel speech enhancement method to reduce the noise and enhance speech at low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) levels and non-stationary noise conditions. Specifically, we focus on modeling the noise using a Gaussian mixture model (GMM) based on a multi-stage process with a parametric Wiener filter. The proposed noise model estimates a more accurate noise power spectral density (PSD), and allows for better generalization under various noise conditions compared to traditional Wiener filtering methods. Simulations show that the proposed approach can achieve better performance in terms of speech quality (PESQ) and intelligibility (STOI) at low SNR levels.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种单通道语音增强方法，用于在低信噪比（SNR）水平和非平稳噪声条件下降低噪声并增强语音。具体来说，我们专注于使用基于参数维纳滤波器的多阶段过程的高斯混合模型 (GMM) 对噪声进行建模。与传统的维纳滤波方法相比，所提出的噪声模型估计了更准确的噪声功率谱密度（PSD），并且可以在各种噪声条件下实现更好的泛化。仿真表明，所提出的方法可以在低 SNR 水平下在语音质量 (PESQ) 和清晰度 (STOI) 方面实现更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于高斯混合模型（GMM）的多阶段维纳滤波方法，用于低信噪比（SNR）下的单通道语音增强。
AI.motivation: 传统维纳滤波在低SNR和非平稳噪声条件下难以准确估计噪声功率谱密度（PSD），导致语音增强效果不佳。
AI.method: 通过多阶段参数维纳滤波结合GMM建模，估计更精确的噪声PSD，提升不同噪声条件下的泛化能力。
AI.result: 仿真结果表明，该方法在低SNR下显著提升语音质量（PESQ）和可懂度（STOI）指标。
AI.conclusion: GMM多阶段维纳滤波有效解决了低SNR和非平稳噪声下的语音增强问题，优于传统方法。

authors_text: Jianfeng Wang,Thomas Lukasiewicz
title: Rethinking Bayesian Deep Learning Methods for Semi-Supervised Volumetric Medical Image Segmentation
title_zh: 重新思考半监督体积医学图像分割的贝叶斯深度学习方法
comment: To appear at CVPR 2022, and the supplementary material can be found at the official site. The source codes are at https://github.com/Jianf-Wang/GBDL
comments: To appear at CVPR 2022, and the supplementary material can be found at the official site. The source codes are at https://github.com/Jianf-Wang/GBDL
abstract: Recently, several Bayesian deep learning methods have been proposed for semi-supervised medical image segmentation. Although they have achieved promising results on medical benchmarks, some problems are still existing. Firstly, their overall architectures belong to the discriminative models, and hence, in the early stage of training, they only use labeled data for training, which might make them overfit to the labeled data. Secondly, in fact, they are only partially based on Bayesian deep learning, as their overall architectures are not designed under the Bayesian framework. However, unifying the overall architecture under the Bayesian perspective can make the architecture have a rigorous theoretical basis, so that each part of the architecture can have a clear probabilistic interpretation. Therefore, to solve the problems, we propose a new generative Bayesian deep learning (GBDL) architecture. GBDL belongs to the generative models, whose target is to estimate the joint distribution of input medical volumes and their corresponding labels. Estimating the joint distribution implicitly involves the distribution of data, so both labeled and unlabeled data can be utilized in the early stage of training, which alleviates the potential overfitting problem. Besides, GBDL is completely designed under the Bayesian framework, and thus we give its full Bayesian formulation, which lays a theoretical probabilistic foundation for our architecture. Extensive experiments show that our GBDL outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods in terms of four commonly used evaluation indicators on three public medical datasets.
summary: Recently, several Bayesian deep learning methods have been proposed for semi-supervised medical image segmentation. Although they have achieved promising results on medical benchmarks, some problems are still existing. Firstly, their overall architectures belong to the discriminative models, and hence, in the early stage of training, they only use labeled data for training, which might make them overfit to the labeled data. Secondly, in fact, they are only partially based on Bayesian deep learning, as their overall architectures are not designed under the Bayesian framework. However, unifying the overall architecture under the Bayesian perspective can make the architecture have a rigorous theoretical basis, so that each part of the architecture can have a clear probabilistic interpretation. Therefore, to solve the problems, we propose a new generative Bayesian deep learning (GBDL) architecture. GBDL belongs to the generative models, whose target is to estimate the joint distribution of input medical volumes and their corresponding labels. Estimating the joint distribution implicitly involves the distribution of data, so both labeled and unlabeled data can be utilized in the early stage of training, which alleviates the potential overfitting problem. Besides, GBDL is completely designed under the Bayesian framework, and thus we give its full Bayesian formulation, which lays a theoretical probabilistic foundation for our architecture. Extensive experiments show that our GBDL outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods in terms of four commonly used evaluation indicators on three public medical datasets.
abstract_zh: 最近，已经提出了几种用于半监督医学图像分割的贝叶斯深度学习方法。尽管他们在医学基准上取得了可喜的成果，但仍然存在一些问题。首先，它们的整体架构属于判别模型，因此，在训练的早期阶段，它们仅使用标记数据进行训练，这可能会使它们过度拟合标记数据。其次，实际上它们只是部分基于贝叶斯深度学习，因为它们的整体架构并不是在贝叶斯框架下设计的。然而，将整体架构统一在贝叶斯视角下，可以使架构拥有严谨的理论基础，从而使架构的每个部分都能有清晰的概率解释。因此，为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种新的生成贝叶斯深度学习（GBDL）架构。 GBDL属于生成模型，其目标是估计输入医疗体及其相应标签的联合分布。估计联合分布隐含地涉及数据的分布，因此在训练的早期阶段可以利用标记数据和未标记数据，从而缓解潜在的过拟合问题。此外，GBDL完全是在贝叶斯框架下设计的，因此我们给出了完整的贝叶斯公式，这为我们的架构奠定了理论概率基础。大量实验表明，我们的 GBDL 在三个公共医疗数据集上的四个常用评估指标方面优于以前最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生成模型的贝叶斯深度学习架构（GBDL），用于解决半监督医学图像分割中的过拟合和理论基础不足问题。
AI.motivation: 现有贝叶斯深度学习方法在医学图像分割中存在过度依赖标注数据和理论框架不严谨的问题，导致模型泛化能力受限。
AI.method: 设计完全基于贝叶斯框架的生成模型GBDL，通过联合估计输入医学影像与标签的联合分布，利用标注和未标注数据共同训练。
AI.result: 在三个公开医学数据集上，GBDL在四个常用评估指标中均优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: GBDL通过生成模型和贝叶斯理论结合，有效缓解过拟合问题并提供严谨的概率解释，为医学图像分割提供新思路。

Although they have achieved promising results on medical benchmarks, some problems are still existing. Firstly, their overall architectures belong to the discriminative models, and hence, in the early stage of training, they only use labeled data for training, which might make them overfit to the labeled data. Secondly, in fact, they are only partially based on Bayesian deep learning, as their overall architectures are not designed under the Bayesian framework. However, unifying the overall architecture under the Bayesian perspective can make the architecture have a rigorous theoretical basis, so that each part of the architecture can have a clear probabilistic interpretation. Therefore, to solve the problems, we propose a new generative Bayesian deep learning (GBDL) architecture. GBDL belongs to the generative models, whose target is to estimate the joint distribution of input medical volumes and their corresponding labels. Estimating the joint distribution implicitly involves the distribution of data, so both labeled and unlabeled data can be utilized in the early stage of training, which alleviates the potential overfitting problem. Besides, GBDL is completely designed under the Bayesian framework, and thus we give its full Bayesian formulation, which lays a theoretical probabilistic foundation for our architecture. Extensive experiments show that our GBDL outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods in terms of four commonly used evaluation indicators on three public medical datasets. 最近，已经提出了几种用于半监督医学图像分割的贝叶斯深度学习方法。尽管他们在医学基准上取得了可喜的成果，但仍然存在一些问题。首先，它们的整体架构属于判别模型，因此，在训练的早期阶段，它们仅使用标记数据进行训练，这可能会使它们过度拟合标记数据。其次，实际上它们只是部分基于贝叶斯深度学习，因为它们的整体架构并不是在贝叶斯框架下设计的。然而，将整体架构统一在贝叶斯视角下，可以使架构拥有严谨的理论基础，从而使架构的每个部分都能有清晰的概率解释。因此，为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种新的生成贝叶斯深度学习（GBDL）架构。 GBDL属于生成模型，其目标是估计输入医疗体及其相应标签的联合分布。估计联合分布隐含地涉及数据的分布，因此在训练的早期阶段可以利用标记数据和未标记数据，从而缓解潜在的过拟合问题。此外，GBDL完全是在贝叶斯框架下设计的，因此我们给出了完整的贝叶斯公式，这为我们的架构奠定了理论概率基础。大量实验表明，我们的 GBDL 在三个公共医疗数据集上的四个常用评估指标方面优于以前最先进的方法。 本文提出一种基于生成模型的贝叶斯深度学习架构（GBDL），用于解决半监督医学图像分割中的过拟合和理论基础不足问题。 Jianfeng Wang,Thomas Lukasiewicz cs.CV

authors_text: Zhengyi Luo,Ye Yuan,Kris M. Kitani
title: From Universal Humanoid Control to Automatic Physically Valid Character Creation
title_zh: 从通用人形控制到自动物理有效角色创建
abstract: Automatically designing virtual humans and humanoids holds great potential in aiding the character creation process in games, movies, and robots. In some cases, a character creator may wish to design a humanoid body customized for certain motions such as karate kicks and parkour jumps. In this work, we propose a humanoid design framework to automatically generate physically valid humanoid bodies conditioned on sequence(s) of pre-specified human motions. First, we learn a generalized humanoid controller trained on a large-scale human motion dataset that features diverse human motion and body shapes. Second, we use a design-and-control framework to optimize a humanoid's physical attributes to find body designs that can better imitate the pre-specified human motion sequence(s). Leveraging the pre-trained humanoid controller and physics simulation as guidance, our method is able to discover new humanoid designs that are customized to perform pre-specified human motions.
summary: Automatically designing virtual humans and humanoids holds great potential in aiding the character creation process in games, movies, and robots. In some cases, a character creator may wish to design a humanoid body customized for certain motions such as karate kicks and parkour jumps. In this work, we propose a humanoid design framework to automatically generate physically valid humanoid bodies conditioned on sequence(s) of pre-specified human motions. First, we learn a generalized humanoid controller trained on a large-scale human motion dataset that features diverse human motion and body shapes. Second, we use a design-and-control framework to optimize a humanoid's physical attributes to find body designs that can better imitate the pre-specified human motion sequence(s). Leveraging the pre-trained humanoid controller and physics simulation as guidance, our method is able to discover new humanoid designs that are customized to perform pre-specified human motions.
abstract_zh: 自动设计虚拟人类和类人机器人在帮助游戏、电影和机器人的角色创建过程方面具有巨大的潜力。在某些情况下，角色创建者可能希望设计针对某些动作（例如空手道踢腿和跑酷跳跃）定制的人形身体。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个人形设计框架，可以根据预先指定的人体运动序列自动生成物理上有效的人形身体。首先，我们学习一个在大规模人体运动数据集上训练的通用人形控制器，该数据集具有不同的人体运动和身体形状。其次，我们使用设计和控制框架来优化人形的物理属性，以找到可以更好地模仿预先指定的人类运动序列的身体设计。利用预先训练的人形控制器和物理模拟作为指导，我们的方法能够发现新的人形设计，这些设计经过定制以执行预先指定的人体运动。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于预设人类运动序列自动生成物理有效人形角色的框架，结合控制学习与物理模拟优化设计。
AI.motivation: 自动设计符合特定动作需求的人形角色在游戏、影视及机器人领域具有重要应用价值，但现有方法难以兼顾物理合理性与动作适配性。
AI.method: 分两阶段：1) 基于大规模人类运动数据集训练通用人形控制器；2) 通过设计-控制联合优化框架，结合预训练控制器与物理模拟，迭代生成适配目标动作的人形结构参数。
AI.result: 成功生成可执行预设动作（如空翻、格斗）的物理有效人形模型，验证了框架在动作驱动角色设计中的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为动作导向的人形角色创作提供了新范式，通过控制学习与物理模拟的协同优化，实现了设计目标与物理可行性的平衡。

authors_text: Nils Bore,John Folkesson
title: Neural Shape-from-Shading for Survey-Scale Self-Consistent Bathymetry from Sidescan
title_zh: 用于从侧扫描进行测量规模自洽水深测量的阴影神经形状
comment: Submitted to IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering (IEEE-JOE), under review as of June 2022
comments: Submitted to IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering (IEEE-JOE), under review as of June 2022
abstract: Sidescan sonar is a small and cost-effective sensing solution that can be easily mounted on most vessels. Historically, it has been used to produce high-definition images that experts may use to identify targets on the seafloor or in the water column. While solutions have been proposed to produce bathymetry solely from sidescan, or in conjunction with multibeam, they have had limited impact. This is partly a result of mostly being limited to single sidescan lines. In this paper, we propose a modern, salable solution to create high quality survey-scale bathymetry from many sidescan lines. By incorporating multiple observations of the same place, results can be improved as the estimates reinforce each other. Our method is based on sinusoidal representation networks, a recent advance in neural representation learning. We demonstrate the scalability of the approach by producing bathymetry from a large sidescan survey. The resulting quality is demonstrated by comparing to data collected with a high-precision multibeam sensor.
summary: Sidescan sonar is a small and cost-effective sensing solution that can be easily mounted on most vessels. Historically, it has been used to produce high-definition images that experts may use to identify targets on the seafloor or in the water column. While solutions have been proposed to produce bathymetry solely from sidescan, or in conjunction with multibeam, they have had limited impact. This is partly a result of mostly being limited to single sidescan lines. In this paper, we propose a modern, salable solution to create high quality survey-scale bathymetry from many sidescan lines. By incorporating multiple observations of the same place, results can be improved as the estimates reinforce each other. Our method is based on sinusoidal representation networks, a recent advance in neural representation learning. We demonstrate the scalability of the approach by producing bathymetry from a large sidescan survey. The resulting quality is demonstrated by comparing to data collected with a high-precision multibeam sensor.
abstract_zh: 侧扫声纳是一种小型且经济高效的传感解决方案，可以轻松安装在大多数船舶上。从历史上看，它曾被用来生成高清图像，专家可以用这些图像来识别海底或水柱中的目标。虽然已经提出了仅通过侧扫或与多波束结合进行测深的解决方案，但它们的影响有限。这部分是由于大多数情况下仅限于单侧扫描线。在本文中，我们提出了一种现代的、可销售的解决方案，用于从许多侧扫描线创建高质量的测量规模测深。通过合并同一地点的多次观测，结果可以得到改善，因为估计值可以相互加强。我们的方法基于正弦表示网络，这是神经表示学习的最新进展。我们通过大型侧扫勘测生成测深数据，展示了该方法的可扩展性。通过与高精度多光束传感器收集的数据进行比较来证明所得的质量。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于神经网络的侧扫声纳数据处理方法，用于生成大范围自洽的海底测深图
AI.motivation: 传统方法受限于单条声纳线，难以生成高质量大范围测深图，需更高效解决方案
AI.method: 基于正弦表示网络的神经形状从阴影方法，整合多条侧扫声纳线数据以提升估计一致性
AI.result: 生成的测深图质量接近高精度多波束数据，验证了方法的有效性
AI.conclusion: 该方法为低成本、大范围海底测绘提供了可行方案，具有实际应用价值

ai_content.conclusion: 该方法为低成本、大范围海底测绘提供了可行方案，具有实际应用价值

authors_text: Lilit Avetisian,Jackie Ayoub,Feng Zhou
title: Anticipated emotions associated with trust in autonomous vehicles
title_zh: 与对自动驾驶汽车的信任相关的预期情绪
abstract: Trust in automation has been mainly studied in the cognitive perspective, though some researchers have shown that trust is also influenced by emotion. Therefore, it is essential to investigate the relationships between emotions and trust. In this study, we explored the pattern of 19 anticipated emotions associated with two levels of trust (i.e., low vs. high levels of trust) elicited from two levels of autonomous vehicles (AVs) performance (i.e., failure and non-failure) from 105 participants from Amazon Mechanical Turk (AMT). Trust was assessed at three layers i.e., dispositional, initial learned, and situational trust. The study was designed to measure how emotions are affected with low and high levels of trust. Situational trust was significantly correlated with emotions that a high level of trust significantly improved participants' positive emotions, and vice versa. We also identified the underlying factors of emotions associated with situational trust. Our results offered important implications on anticipated emotions associated with trust in AVs.
summary: Trust in automation has been mainly studied in the cognitive perspective, though some researchers have shown that trust is also influenced by emotion. Therefore, it is essential to investigate the relationships between emotions and trust. In this study, we explored the pattern of 19 anticipated emotions associated with two levels of trust (i.e., low vs. high levels of trust) elicited from two levels of autonomous vehicles (AVs) performance (i.e., failure and non-failure) from 105 participants from Amazon Mechanical Turk (AMT). Trust was assessed at three layers i.e., dispositional, initial learned, and situational trust. The study was designed to measure how emotions are affected with low and high levels of trust. Situational trust was significantly correlated with emotions that a high level of trust significantly improved participants' positive emotions, and vice versa. We also identified the underlying factors of emotions associated with situational trust. Our results offered important implications on anticipated emotions associated with trust in AVs.
abstract_zh: 对自动化的信任主要从认知角度进行研究，尽管一些研究人员表明信任也会受到情感的影响。因此，有必要研究情感与信任之间的关系。在这项研究中，我们探索了与两个信任级别（即低信任度和高信任度）相关的 19 种预期情绪模式，这些情绪是从 Amazon Mechanical Turk (AMT) 的 105 名参与者的两个级别的自动驾驶车辆 (AV) 性能（即失败和未失败）中得出的。信任分为三个层面进行评估，即倾向性信任、初始学习性信任和情境信任。该研究旨在衡量低信任度和高信任度对情绪的影响。情境信任与情绪显着相关，高水平的信任显着改善参与者的积极情绪，反之亦然。我们还确定了与情境信任相关的情绪的潜在因素。我们的结果对与自动驾驶信任相关的预期情绪提供了重要影响。

AI.tldr: 本研究探讨了自动驾驶汽车（AV）不同性能水平（失败与非失败）引发的低信任与高信任状态下，19种预期情绪的模式及其与信任的关系。
AI.motivation: 尽管信任自动化系统主要从认知角度研究，但已有研究表明情绪也会影响信任。因此，需进一步探究情绪与信任之间的关系，以完善对自动驾驶汽车中信任机制的理解。
AI.method: 通过Amazon Mechanical Turk招募105名参与者，基于AV性能（失败/非失败）和信任水平（低/高），测量三种信任层次（特质性、初始习得性、情境性信任）下的情绪反应，并分析情绪与信任的关联。
AI.result: 情境性信任与情绪显著相关：高信任显著提升积极情绪，低信任则增强消极情绪。研究还识别了与情境性信任相关的情绪潜在因素。
AI.conclusion: 情绪在自动驾驶汽车信任形成中起关键作用，研究结果为设计提升用户信任的AV系统提供了情绪层面的重要启示。

ai_content.tldr: 本研究探讨了自动驾驶汽车（AV）不同性能水平（失败与非失败）引发的低信任与高信任状态下，19种预期情绪的模式及其与信任的关系。

search_text: Anticipated emotions associated with trust in autonomous vehicles 与对自动驾驶汽车的信任相关的预期情绪 Trust in automation has been mainly studied in the cognitive perspective, though some researchers have shown that trust is also influenced by emotion. Therefore, it is essential to investigate the relationships between emotions and trust. In this study, we explored the pattern of 19 anticipated emotions associated with two levels of trust (i.e., low vs. high levels of trust) elicited from two levels of autonomous vehicles (AVs) performance (i.e., failure and non-failure) from 105 participants from Amazon Mechanical Turk (AMT). Trust was assessed at three layers i.e., dispositional, initial learned, and situational trust. The study was designed to measure how emotions are affected with low and high levels of trust. Situational trust was significantly correlated with emotions that a high level of trust significantly improved participants' positive emotions, and vice versa. We also identified the underlying factors of emotions associated with situational trust. Our results offered important implications on anticipated emotions associated with trust in AVs. 对自动化的信任主要从认知角度进行研究，尽管一些研究人员表明信任也会受到情感的影响。因此，有必要研究情感与信任之间的关系。在这项研究中，我们探索了与两个信任级别（即低信任度和高信任度）相关的 19 种预期情绪模式，这些情绪是从 Amazon Mechanical Turk (AMT) 的 105 名参与者的两个级别的自动驾驶车辆 (AV) 性能（即失败和未失败）中得出的。信任分为三个层面进行评估，即倾向性信任、初始学习性信任和情境信任。该研究旨在衡量低信任度和高信任度对情绪的影响。情境信任与情绪显着相关，高水平的信任显着改善参与者的积极情绪，反之亦然。我们还确定了与情境信任相关的情绪的潜在因素。我们的结果对与自动驾驶信任相关的预期情绪提供了重要影响。 本研究探讨了自动驾驶汽车（AV）不同性能水平（失败与非失败）引发的低信任与高信任状态下，19种预期情绪的模式及其与信任的关系。 Lilit Avetisian,Jackie Ayoub,Feng Zhou cs.HC

authors_text: Akarsh Prabhakara,Tao Jin,Arnav Das,Gantavya Bhatt,Lilly Kumari,Elahe Soltanaghaei,Jeff Bilmes,Swarun Kumar,Anthony Rowe
title: High Resolution Point Clouds from mmWave Radar
title_zh: 毫米波雷达的高分辨率点云
comment: ef:2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA), London, United Kingdom, 2023, pp. 4135-4142
comments: ef:2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA), London, United Kingdom, 2023, pp. 4135-4142
abstract: This paper explores a machine learning approach for generating high resolution point clouds from a single-chip mmWave radar. Unlike lidar and vision-based systems, mmWave radar can operate in harsh environments and see through occlusions like smoke, fog, and dust. Unfortunately, current mmWave processing techniques offer poor spatial resolution compared to lidar point clouds. This paper presents RadarHD, an end-to-end neural network that constructs lidar-like point clouds from low resolution radar input. Enhancing radar images is challenging due to the presence of specular and spurious reflections. Radar data also doesn't map well to traditional image processing techniques due to the signal's sinc-like spreading pattern. We overcome these challenges by training RadarHD on a large volume of raw I/Q radar data paired with lidar point clouds across diverse indoor settings. Our experiments show the ability to generate rich point clouds even in scenes unobserved during training and in the presence of heavy smoke occlusion. Further, RadarHD's point clouds are high-quality enough to work with existing lidar odometry and mapping workflows.
summary: This paper explores a machine learning approach for generating high resolution point clouds from a single-chip mmWave radar. Unlike lidar and vision-based systems, mmWave radar can operate in harsh environments and see through occlusions like smoke, fog, and dust. Unfortunately, current mmWave processing techniques offer poor spatial resolution compared to lidar point clouds. This paper presents RadarHD, an end-to-end neural network that constructs lidar-like point clouds from low resolution radar input. Enhancing radar images is challenging due to the presence of specular and spurious reflections. Radar data also doesn't map well to traditional image processing techniques due to the signal's sinc-like spreading pattern. We overcome these challenges by training RadarHD on a large volume of raw I/Q radar data paired with lidar point clouds across diverse indoor settings. Our experiments show the ability to generate rich point clouds even in scenes unobserved during training and in the presence of heavy smoke occlusion. Further, RadarHD's point clouds are high-quality enough to work with existing lidar odometry and mapping workflows.
abstract_zh: 本文探讨了一种从单芯片毫米波雷达生成高分辨率点云的机器学习方法。与激光雷达和基于视觉的系统不同，毫米波雷达可以在恶劣的环境中运行，并能穿透烟雾、雾气和灰尘等遮挡物。不幸的是，与激光雷达点云相比，当前毫米波处理技术的空间分辨率较差。本文介绍了 RadarHD，这是一种端到端神经网络，可根据低分辨率雷达输入构建类似激光雷达的点云。由于镜面反射和杂散反射的存在，增强雷达图像具有挑战性。由于信号具有类似 sinc 的扩展模式，雷达数据也不能很好地映射到传统图像处理技术。我们通过使用大量原始 I/Q 雷达数据与不同室内环境中的激光雷达点云进行训练 RadarHD，克服了这些挑战。我们的实验表明，即使在训练期间未观察到的场景以及存在浓烟遮挡的情况下，也能够生成丰富的点云。此外，RadarHD 的点云质量足够高，可以与现有的激光雷达里程计和测绘工作流程配合使用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于机器学习的方法，通过单芯片毫米波雷达生成高分辨率点云。
AI.motivation: 毫米波雷达相比激光雷达和视觉系统能在恶劣环境中工作，但现有技术空间分辨率不足，需提升其点云质量以满足实际需求。
AI.method: 设计端到端神经网络RadarHD，利用大量原始I/Q雷达数据与激光雷达点云配对训练，克服镜面反射和信号扩散等问题。
AI.result: 生成的点云在未观测场景及浓烟遮挡下仍保持高质量，可直接用于现有激光雷达定位与建图流程。
AI.conclusion: RadarHD有效提升了毫米波雷达点云分辨率，为复杂环境下的感知系统提供了新解决方案。

We overcome these challenges by training RadarHD on a large volume of raw I/Q radar data paired with lidar point clouds across diverse indoor settings. Our experiments show the ability to generate rich point clouds even in scenes unobserved during training and in the presence of heavy smoke occlusion. authors_text: Guanhong Tao,Yingqi Liu,Siyuan Cheng,Shengwei An,Zhuo Zhang,Qiuling Xu,Guangyu Shen,Xiangyu Zhang
title: DECK: Model Hardening for Defending Pervasive Backdoors
title_zh: DECK：用于防御普遍后门的模型强化
abstract: Pervasive backdoors are triggered by dynamic and pervasive input perturbations. They can be intentionally injected by attackers or naturally exist in normally trained models. They have a different nature from the traditional static and localized backdoors that can be triggered by perturbing a small input area with some fixed pattern, e.g., a patch with solid color. Existing defense techniques are highly effective for traditional backdoors. However, they may not work well for pervasive backdoors, especially regarding backdoor removal and model hardening. In this paper, we propose a novel model hardening technique against pervasive backdoors, including both natural and injected backdoors. We develop a general pervasive attack based on an encoder-decoder architecture enhanced with a special transformation layer. The attack can model a wide range of existing pervasive backdoor attacks and quantify them by class distances. As such, using the samples derived from our attack in adversarial training can harden a model against these backdoor vulnerabilities. Our evaluation on 9 datasets with 15 model structures shows that our technique can enlarge class distances by 59.65% on average with less than 1% accuracy degradation and no robustness loss, outperforming five hardening techniques such as adversarial training, universal adversarial training, MOTH, etc. It can reduce the attack success rate of six pervasive backdoor attacks from 99.06% to 1.94%, surpassing seven state-of-the-art backdoor removal techniques.
summary: Pervasive backdoors are triggered by dynamic and pervasive input perturbations. They can be intentionally injected by attackers or naturally exist in normally trained models. They have a different nature from the traditional static and localized backdoors that can be triggered by perturbing a small input area with some fixed pattern, e.g., a patch with solid color. Existing defense techniques are highly effective for traditional backdoors. However, they may not work well for pervasive backdoors, especially regarding backdoor removal and model hardening. In this paper, we propose a novel model hardening technique against pervasive backdoors, including both natural and injected backdoors. We develop a general pervasive attack based on an encoder-decoder architecture enhanced with a special transformation layer. The attack can model a wide range of existing pervasive backdoor attacks and quantify them by class distances. As such, using the samples derived from our attack in adversarial training can harden a model against these backdoor vulnerabilities. Our evaluation on 9 datasets with 15 model structures shows that our technique can enlarge class distances by 59.65% on average with less than 1% accuracy degradation and no robustness loss, outperforming five hardening techniques such as adversarial training, universal adversarial training, MOTH, etc. It can reduce the attack success rate of six pervasive backdoor attacks from 99.06% to 1.94%, surpassing seven state-of-the-art backdoor removal techniques.
abstract_zh: 普遍的后门是由动态和普遍的输入扰动触发的。它们可以由攻击者故意注入，也可以自然存在于正常训练的模型中。它们与传统的静态和局部后门具有不同的性质，传统的静态和局部后门可以通过扰乱具有某种固定模式（例如纯色补丁）的小输入区域来触发。现有的防御技术对于传统后门来说非常有效。然而，它们可能不适用于普遍存在的后门，特别是在后门删除和模型强化方面。在本文中，我们提出了一种针对普遍后门（包括自然后门和注入后门）的新型模型强化技术。我们开发了一种基于编码器-解码器架构的通用攻击，该架构通过特殊的转换层进行了增强。该攻击可以对各种现有的普遍后门攻击进行建模，并按类别距离对其进行量化。因此，在对抗训练中使用从我们的攻击中获得的样本可以强化针对这些后门漏洞的模型。我们对 9 个数据集、15 个模型结构的评估表明，我们的技术可以将类别距离平均扩大 59.65%，准确率下降不到 1%，并且没有鲁棒性损失，优于对抗训练、通用对抗训练、MOTH 等五种强化技术。它可以将六种普遍后门攻击的攻击成功率从 99.06% 降低到 1.94%，超过七种最先进的后门去除技术技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DECK模型加固技术，用于防御普遍存在的后门攻击，包括自然和注入的后门。
AI.motivation: 现有防御技术对传统静态后门有效，但难以应对动态且广泛存在的后门攻击，尤其在后门移除和模型加固方面存在不足。
AI.method: 基于编码器-解码器架构增强特殊变换层，构建通用攻击模型，通过对抗训练生成样本以增强模型鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在9个数据集和15种模型结构上，平均扩大类别距离59.65%（精度损失<1%），将六种普遍后门攻击成功率从99.06%降至1.94%，优于七种最先进移除技术。
AI.conclusion: DECK通过对抗训练有效增强模型对普遍后门的防御能力，在保持模型准确性的同时显著提升鲁棒性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出DECK模型加固技术，用于防御普遍存在的后门攻击，包括自然和注入的后门。

search_text: DECK: Model Hardening for Defending Pervasive Backdoors DECK：用于防御普遍后门的模型强化 Pervasive backdoors are triggered by dynamic and pervasive input perturbations. They can be intentionally injected by attackers or naturally exist in normally trained models. They have a different nature from the traditional static and localized backdoors that can be triggered by perturbing a small input area with some fixed pattern, e.g., a patch with solid color. Existing defense techniques are highly effective for traditional backdoors. However, they may not work well for pervasive backdoors, especially regarding backdoor removal and model hardening. In this paper, we propose a novel model hardening technique against pervasive backdoors, including both natural and injected backdoors. We develop a general pervasive attack based on an encoder-decoder architecture enhanced with a special transformation layer. The attack can model a wide range of existing pervasive backdoor attacks and quantify them by class distances. As such, using the samples derived from our attack in adversarial training can harden a model against these backdoor vulnerabilities. Our evaluation on 9 datasets with 15 model structures shows that our technique can enlarge class distances by 59.65% on average with less than 1% accuracy degradation and no robustness loss, outperforming five hardening techniques such as adversarial training, universal adversarial training, MOTH, etc. It can reduce the attack success rate of six pervasive backdoor attacks from 99.06% to 1.94%, surpassing seven state-of-the-art backdoor removal techniques. 普遍的后门是由动态和普遍的输入扰动触发的。它们可以由攻击者故意注入，也可以自然存在于正常训练的模型中。它们与传统的静态和局部后门具有不同的性质，传统的静态和局部后门可以通过扰乱具有某种固定模式（例如纯色补丁）的小输入区域来触发。现有的防御技术对于传统后门来说非常有效。然而，它们可能不适用于普遍存在的后门，特别是在后门删除和模型强化方面。在本文中，我们提出了一种针对普遍后门（包括自然后门和注入后门）的新型模型强化技术。我们开发了一种基于编码器-解码器架构的通用攻击，该架构通过特殊的转换层进行了增强。该攻击可以对各种现有的普遍后门攻击进行建模，并按类别距离对其进行量化。因此，在对抗训练中使用从我们的攻击中获得的样本可以强化针对这些后门漏洞的模型。我们对 9 个数据集、15 个模型结构的评估表明，我们的技术可以将类别距离平均扩大 59.65%，准确率下降不到 1%，并且没有鲁棒性损失，优于对抗训练、通用对抗训练、MOTH 等五种强化技术。它可以将六种普遍后门攻击的攻击成功率从 99.06% 降低到 1.94%，超过七种最先进的后门去除技术技术。 本文提出DECK模型加固技术，用于防御普遍存在的后门攻击，包括自然和注入的后门。 Guanhong Tao,Yingqi Liu,Siyuan Cheng,Shengwei An,Zhuo Zhang,Qiuling Xu,Guangyu Shen,Xiangyu Zhang cs.CR,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Pritish Sahu,Kalliopi Basioti,Vladimir Pavlovic
title: SAViR-T: Spatially Attentive Visual Reasoning with Transformers
title_zh: SAViR-T：使用 Transformers 进行空间注意力视觉推理
abstract: We present a novel computational model, "SAViR-T", for the family of visual reasoning problems embodied in the Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPM). Our model considers explicit spatial semantics of visual elements within each image in the puzzle, encoded as spatio-visual tokens, and learns the intra-image as well as the inter-image token dependencies, highly relevant for the visual reasoning task. Token-wise relationship, modeled through a transformer-based SAViR-T architecture, extract group (row or column) driven representations by leveraging the group-rule coherence and use this as the inductive bias to extract the underlying rule representations in the top two row (or column) per token in the RPM. We use this relation representations to locate the correct choice image that completes the last row or column for the RPM. Extensive experiments across both synthetic RPM benchmarks, including RAVEN, I-RAVEN, RAVEN-FAIR, and PGM, and the natural image-based "V-PROM" demonstrate that SAViR-T sets a new state-of-the-art for visual reasoning, exceeding prior models' performance by a considerable margin.
summary: We present a novel computational model, "SAViR-T", for the family of visual reasoning problems embodied in the Raven's Progressive Matrices (RPM). Our model considers explicit spatial semantics of visual elements within each image in the puzzle, encoded as spatio-visual tokens, and learns the intra-image as well as the inter-image token dependencies, highly relevant for the visual reasoning task. Token-wise relationship, modeled through a transformer-based SAViR-T architecture, extract group (row or column) driven representations by leveraging the group-rule coherence and use this as the inductive bias to extract the underlying rule representations in the top two row (or column) per token in the RPM. We use this relation representations to locate the correct choice image that completes the last row or column for the RPM. Extensive experiments across both synthetic RPM benchmarks, including RAVEN, I-RAVEN, RAVEN-FAIR, and PGM, and the natural image-based "V-PROM" demonstrate that SAViR-T sets a new state-of-the-art for visual reasoning, exceeding prior models' performance by a considerable margin.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的计算模型“SAViR-T”，用于解决乌鸦渐进矩阵（RPM）中体现的视觉推理问题系列。我们的模型考虑拼图中每个图像内视觉元素的显式空间语义，编码为空间视觉标记，并学习图像内以及图像间标记依赖性，与视觉推理任务高度相关。通过基于变压器的 SAViR-T 架构建模的令牌明智关系，通过利用组规则一致性来提取组（行或列）驱动的表示，并将其用作归纳偏差来提取 RPM 中每个令牌的前两行（或列）中的底层规则表示。我们使用这种关系表示来定位完成 RPM 最后一行或一列的正确选择图像。跨两个合成 RPM 基准（包括 RAVEN、I-RAVEN、RAVEN-FAIR 和 PGM）以及基于自然图像的“V-PROM”的大量实验表明，SAViR-T 为视觉推理设定了新的最先进技术，大大超过了先前模型的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为SAViR-T的新型计算模型，用于解决类比推理问题，特别是针对Raven's Progressive Matrices（RPM）任务，通过空间注意力机制和Transformer架构提升视觉推理能力。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉推理模型在处理RPM任务时未能充分捕捉图像中视觉元素的空间语义关系，导致推理效果受限。本文旨在通过显式建模空间依赖性来提升模型性能。
AI.method: 将图像中的视觉元素编码为包含空间信息的token，利用Transformer架构建模token间的局部（行/列）依赖关系，并通过组规则一致性提取高层规则表示，最终定位完成矩阵的正确选项。
AI.result: 在合成数据集（RAVEN、I-RAVEN等）和真实图像数据集（V-PROM）上均取得显著优于现有方法的性能，刷新了视觉推理任务的最先进水平。
AI.conclusion: SAViR-T通过显式空间语义建模和Transformer的全局依赖捕捉能力，有效解决了RPM任务中的复杂视觉推理问题，为该领域提供了新的技术方向。

authors_text: Abhinav Lalwani,Aman Saraiya,Apoorv Singh,Aditya Jain,Tirtharaj Dash
title: Machine Learning in Sports: A Case Study on Using Explainable Models for Predicting Outcomes of Volleyball Matches
title_zh: 体育中的机器学习：使用可解释模型预测排球比赛结果的案例研究
comment: 9 pages, 1 figure, accepted to 2nd International Conference on Sports Engineering (ICSE 2021)
comments: 9 pages, 1 figure, accepted to 2nd International Conference on Sports Engineering (ICSE 2021)
abstract: Machine Learning has become an integral part of engineering design and decision making in several domains, including sports. Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) have been the state-of-the-art methods for predicting outcomes of professional sports events. However, apart from getting highly accurate predictions on these sports events outcomes, it is necessary to answer questions such as "Why did the model predict that Team A would win Match X against Team B?" DNNs are inherently black-box in nature. Therefore, it is required to provide high-quality interpretable, and understandable explanations for a model's prediction in sports. This paper explores a two-phased Explainable Artificial Intelligence(XAI) approach to predict outcomes of matches in the Brazilian volleyball League (SuperLiga). In the first phase, we directly use the interpretable rule-based ML models that provide a global understanding of the model's behaviors based on Boolean Rule Column Generation (BRCG; extracts simple AND-OR classification rules) and Logistic Regression (LogReg; allows to estimate the feature importance scores). In the second phase, we construct non-linear models such as Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Deep Neural Network (DNN) to obtain predictive performance on the volleyball matches' outcomes. We construct the "post-hoc" explanations for each data instance using ProtoDash, a method that finds prototypes in the training dataset that are most similar to the test instance, and SHAP, a method that estimates the contribution of each feature on the model's prediction. We evaluate the SHAP explanations using the faithfulness metric. Our results demonstrate the effectiveness of the explanations for the model's predictions.
summary: Machine Learning has become an integral part of engineering design and decision making in several domains, including sports. Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) have been the state-of-the-art methods for predicting outcomes of professional sports events. However, apart from getting highly accurate predictions on these sports events outcomes, it is necessary to answer questions such as "Why did the model predict that Team A would win Match X against Team B?" DNNs are inherently black-box in nature. Therefore, it is required to provide high-quality interpretable, and understandable explanations for a model's prediction in sports. This paper explores a two-phased Explainable Artificial Intelligence(XAI) approach to predict outcomes of matches in the Brazilian volleyball League (SuperLiga). In the first phase, we directly use the interpretable rule-based ML models that provide a global understanding of the model's behaviors based on Boolean Rule Column Generation (BRCG; extracts simple AND-OR classification rules) and Logistic Regression (LogReg; allows to estimate the feature importance scores). In the second phase, we construct non-linear models such as Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Deep Neural Network (DNN) to obtain predictive performance on the volleyball matches' outcomes. We construct the "post-hoc" explanations for each data instance using ProtoDash, a method that finds prototypes in the training dataset that are most similar to the test instance, and SHAP, a method that estimates the contribution of each feature on the model's prediction. We evaluate the SHAP explanations using the faithfulness metric. Our results demonstrate the effectiveness of the explanations for the model's predictions.
abstract_zh: 机器学习已成为包括体育在内的多个领域的工程设计和决策不可或缺的一部分。深度神经网络 (DNN) 是预测职业体育赛事结果的最先进方法。然而，除了对这些体育赛事结果进行高度准确的预测之外，还需要回答诸如“为什么模型预测 A 队会赢得 B 队 X 场比赛？”之类的问题。 DNN 本质上是黑盒。因此，需要为体育模型的预测提供高质量的可解释和可理解的解释。本文探讨了一种两阶段的可解释人工智能 (XAI) 方法来预测巴西排球联赛 (SuperLiga) 的比赛结果。在第一阶段，我们直接使用可解释的基于规则的 ML 模型，该模型基于布尔规则列生成（BRCG；提取简单的 AND-OR 分类规则）和逻辑回归（LogReg；允许估计特征重要性分数）提供对模型行为的全局理解。在第二阶段，我们构建非线性模型，例如支持向量机（SVM）和深度神经网络（DNN），以获得排球比赛结果的预测性能。我们使用 ProtoDash（一种在训练数据集中查找与测试实例最相似的原型的方法）和 SHAP（一种估计每个特征对模型预测的贡献的方法）为每个数据实例构建“事后”解释。我们使用忠实度度量来评估 SHAP 解释。我们的结果证明了模型预测解释的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了在巴西排球联赛中使用可解释人工智能（XAI）方法预测比赛结果，结合可解释模型与非线性模型以提高预测透明度。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络（DNN）虽能准确预测体育赛事结果，但其黑箱特性难以解释预测依据，需提供可理解的解释以增强可信度。
AI.method: 采用两阶段XAI方法：第一阶段使用布尔规则列生成（BRCG）和逻辑回归（LogReg）建立可解释模型；第二阶段通过支持向量机（SVM）和DNN提升预测性能，并利用ProtoDash和SHAP进行事后解释。
AI.result: 实验表明，基于SHAP的解释通过保真度指标验证有效，能够清晰展示特征对预测的贡献。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保证预测准确性的同时，通过可解释模型和事后分析技术有效提升了体育赛事预测的透明度与可信度。

ai_content_json.method: 采用两阶段XAI方法：第一阶段使用布尔规则列生成（BRCG）和逻辑回归（LogReg）建立可解释模型；第二阶段通过支持向量机（SVM）和DNN提升预测性能，并利用ProtoDash和SHAP进行事后解释。
ai_content_json.result: 实验表明，基于SHAP的解释通过保真度指标验证有效，能够清晰展示特征对预测的贡献。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 该方法在保证预测准确性的同时，通过可解释模型和事后分析技术有效提升了体育赛事预测的透明度与可信度。

tldr: 本文探讨了在巴西排球联赛中使用可解释人工智能（XAI）方法预测比赛结果，结合可解释模型与非线性模型以提高预测透明度。
motivation: 深度神经网络（DNN）虽能准确预测体育赛事结果，但其黑箱特性难以解释预测依据，需提供可理解的解释以增强可信度。
method: 采用两阶段XAI方法：第一阶段使用布尔规则列生成（BRCG）和逻辑回归（LogReg）建立可解释模型；第二阶段通过支持向量机（SVM）和DNN提升预测性能，并利用ProtoDash和SHAP进行事后解释。
result: 实验表明，基于SHAP的解释通过保真度指标验证有效，能够清晰展示特征对预测的贡献。
conclusion: 该方法在保证预测准确性的同时，通过可解释模型和事后分析技术有效提升了体育赛事预测的透明度与可信度。
search_text: Machine Learning in Sports: A Case Study on Using Explainable Models for Predicting Outcomes of Volleyball Matches 体育中的机器学习：使用可解释模型预测排球比赛结果的案例研究 Machine Learning has become an integral part of engineering design and decision making in several domains, including sports. Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) have been the state-of-the-art methods for predicting outcomes of professional sports events. However, apart from getting highly accurate predictions on these sports events outcomes, it is necessary to answer questions such as "Why did the model predict that Team A would win Match X against Team B?" DNNs are inherently black-box in nature. Therefore, it is required to provide high-quality interpretable, and understandable explanations for a model's prediction in sports. This paper explores a two-phased Explainable Artificial Intelligence(XAI) approach to predict outcomes of matches in the Brazilian volleyball League (SuperLiga). In the first phase, we directly use the interpretable rule-based ML models that provide a global understanding of the model's behaviors based on Boolean Rule Column Generation (BRCG; extracts simple AND-OR classification rules) and Logistic Regression (LogReg; allows to estimate the feature importance scores). In the second phase, we construct non-linear models such as Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Deep Neural Network (DNN) to obtain predictive performance on the volleyball matches' outcomes. We construct the "post-hoc" explanations for each data instance using ProtoDash, a method that finds prototypes in the training dataset that are most similar to the test instance, and SHAP, a method that estimates the contribution of each feature on the model's prediction. We evaluate the SHAP explanations using the faithfulness metric. Our results demonstrate the effectiveness of the explanations for the model's predictions. 机器学习已成为包括体育在内的多个领域的工程设计和决策不可或缺的一部分。深度神经网络 (DNN) 是预测职业体育赛事结果的最先进方法。然而，除了对这些体育赛事结果进行高度准确的预测之外，还需要回答诸如“为什么模型预测 A 队会赢得 B 队 X 场比赛？”之类的问题。 DNN 本质上是黑盒。因此，需要为体育模型的预测提供高质量的可解释和可理解的解释。本文探讨了一种两阶段的可解释人工智能 (XAI) 方法来预测巴西排球联赛 (SuperLiga) 的比赛结果。在第一阶段，我们直接使用可解释的基于规则的 ML 模型，该模型基于布尔规则列生成（BRCG；提取简单的 AND-OR 分类规则）和逻辑回归（LogReg；允许估计特征重要性分数）提供对模型行为的全局理解。在第二阶段，我们构建非线性模型，例如支持向量机（SVM）和深度神经网络（DNN），以获得排球比赛结果的预测性能。我们使用 ProtoDash（一种在训练数据集中查找与测试实例最相似的原型的方法）和 SHAP（一种估计每个特征对模型预测的贡献的方法）为每个数据实例构建“事后”解释。我们使用忠实度度量来评估 SHAP 解释。我们的结果证明了模型预测解释的有效性。 本文探讨了在巴西排球联赛中使用可解释人工智能（XAI）方法预测比赛结果，结合可解释模型与非线性模型以提高预测透明度。 Abhinav Lalwani,Aman Saraiya,Apoorv Singh,Aditya Jain,Tirtharaj Dash cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Zunayed Mahmud,Paul Hungler,Ali Etemad
title: Multistream Gaze Estimation with Anatomical Eye Region Isolation by Synthetic to Real Transfer Learning
title_zh: 通过综合到真实的迁移学习进行多流注视估计和解剖眼区域隔离
comment: 15 pages, 7 figures, 14 tables. This work has been accepted to the IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence $©$ 2024 IEEE. Personal use of this material is permitted. Permission from IEEE must be obtained for all other uses
comments: 15 pages, 7 figures, 14 tables. This work has been accepted to the IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence $©$ 2024 IEEE. Personal use of this material is permitted. Permission from IEEE must be obtained for all other uses
abstract: We propose a novel neural pipeline, MSGazeNet, that learns gaze representations by taking advantage of the eye anatomy information through a multistream framework. Our proposed solution comprises two components, first a network for isolating anatomical eye regions, and a second network for multistream gaze estimation. The eye region isolation is performed with a U-Net style network which we train using a synthetic dataset that contains eye region masks for the visible eyeball and the iris region. The synthetic dataset used in this stage is procured using the UnityEyes simulator, and consists of 80,000 eye images. Successive to training, the eye region isolation network is then transferred to the real domain for generating masks for the real-world eye images. In order to successfully make the transfer, we exploit domain randomization in the training process, which allows for the synthetic images to benefit from a larger variance with the help of augmentations that resemble artifacts. The generated eye region masks along with the raw eye images are then used together as a multistream input to our gaze estimation network, which consists of wide residual blocks. The output embeddings from these encoders are fused in the channel dimension before feeding into the gaze regression layers. We evaluate our framework on three gaze estimation datasets and achieve strong performances. Our method surpasses the state-of-the-art by 7.57% and 1.85% on two datasets, and obtains competitive results on the other. We also study the robustness of our method with respect to the noise in the data and demonstrate that our model is less sensitive to noisy data. Lastly, we perform a variety of experiments including ablation studies to evaluate the contribution of different components and design choices in our solution.
summary: We propose a novel neural pipeline, MSGazeNet, that learns gaze representations by taking advantage of the eye anatomy information through a multistream framework. Our proposed solution comprises two components, first a network for isolating anatomical eye regions, and a second network for multistream gaze estimation. The eye region isolation is performed with a U-Net style network which we train using a synthetic dataset that contains eye region masks for the visible eyeball and the iris region. The synthetic dataset used in this stage is procured using the UnityEyes simulator, and consists of 80,000 eye images. Successive to training, the eye region isolation network is then transferred to the real domain for generating masks for the real-world eye images. In order to successfully make the transfer, we exploit domain randomization in the training process, which allows for the synthetic images to benefit from a larger variance with the help of augmentations that resemble artifacts. The generated eye region masks along with the raw eye images are then used together as a multistream input to our gaze estimation network, which consists of wide residual blocks. The output embeddings from these encoders are fused in the channel dimension before feeding into the gaze regression layers. We evaluate our framework on three gaze estimation datasets and achieve strong performances. Our method surpasses the state-of-the-art by 7.57% and 1.85% on two datasets, and obtains competitive results on the other. We also study the robustness of our method with respect to the noise in the data and demonstrate that our model is less sensitive to noisy data. Lastly, we perform a variety of experiments including ablation studies to evaluate the contribution of different components and design choices in our solution.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的神经管道 MSGazeNet，它通过多流框架利用眼睛解剖信息来学习凝视表示。我们提出的解决方案包括两个组件，第一个用于隔离眼睛解剖区域的网络，第二个用于多流注视估计的网络。眼睛区域隔离是通过 U-Net 风格的网络执行的，我们使用包含可见眼球和虹膜区域的眼睛区域掩模的合成数据集进行训练。此阶段使用的合成数据集是使用 UnityEyes 模拟器获取的，由 80,000 个眼睛图像组成。训练结束后，眼睛区域隔离网络被转移到真实域，为真实世界的眼睛图像生成掩模。为了成功地进行转移，我们在训练过程中利用域随机化，这使得合成图像能够借助类似于伪影的增强从更大的方差中受益。然后，生成的眼睛区域掩模与原始眼睛图像一起用作我们的注视估计网络的多流输入，该网络由宽残差块组成。这些编码器的输出嵌入在输入凝视回归层之前在通道维度中融合。我们在三个注视估计数据集上评估我们的框架并取得了出色的性能。我们的方法在两个数据集上超越了最先进的方法 7.57% 和 1.85%，并在另一个数据集上获得了有竞争力的结果。我们还研究了我们的方法对于数据中的噪声的鲁棒性，并证明我们的模型对噪声数据不太敏感。最后，我们进行了各种实验，包括消融研究，以评估不同组件和设计选择在我们的解决方案中的贡献。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于合成到真实迁移学习的多流眼动估计方法，通过解剖学眼区隔离提升估计精度。
AI.motivation: 现有眼动估计方法未能有效利用眼部解剖结构信息，且在真实场景中易受噪声和域差异影响。
AI.method: 设计MSGazeNet双网络框架：1) 使用U-Net结构结合UnityEyes合成数据训练眼区分割模型；2) 通过域随机化迁移至真实数据，将分割掩码与原始图像作为多流输入，结合宽残差块和通道级特征融合进行眼动回归。
AI.result: 在三个数据集上分别超越SOTA方法7.57%、1.85%，并在噪声环境下展现更强鲁棒性，消融实验证实方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 合成到真实迁移学习结合解剖结构信息显著提升了眼动估计性能，为跨域视觉任务提供新思路。

We also study the robustness of our method with respect to the noise in the data and demonstrate that our model is less sensitive to noisy data. authors_text: Harsh Vardhan,Janos Sztipanovits
title: Reduced Robust Random Cut Forest for Out-Of-Distribution detection in machine learning models
title_zh: 用于机器学习模型中分布外检测的减少鲁棒随机切割森林
abstract: Most machine learning-based regressors extract information from data collected via past observations of limited length to make predictions in the future. Consequently, when input to these trained models is data with significantly different statistical properties from data used for training, there is no guarantee of accurate prediction. Consequently, using these models on out-of-distribution input data may result in a completely different predicted outcome from the desired one, which is not only erroneous but can also be hazardous in some cases. Successful deployment of these machine learning models in any system requires a detection system, which should be able to distinguish between out-of-distribution and in-distribution data (i.e. similar to training data). In this paper, we introduce a novel approach for this detection process using a Reduced Robust Random Cut Forest (RRRCF) data structure, which can be used on both small and large data sets. Similar to the Robust Random Cut Forest (RRCF), RRRCF is a structured, but a reduced representation of the training data sub-space in form of cut trees. Empirical results of this method on both low and high-dimensional data showed that inference about data being in/out of training distribution can be made efficiently and the model is easy to train with no difficult hyper-parameter tuning. The paper discusses two different use-cases for testing and validating results.
summary: Most machine learning-based regressors extract information from data collected via past observations of limited length to make predictions in the future. Consequently, when input to these trained models is data with significantly different statistical properties from data used for training, there is no guarantee of accurate prediction. Consequently, using these models on out-of-distribution input data may result in a completely different predicted outcome from the desired one, which is not only erroneous but can also be hazardous in some cases. Successful deployment of these machine learning models in any system requires a detection system, which should be able to distinguish between out-of-distribution and in-distribution data (i.e. similar to training data). In this paper, we introduce a novel approach for this detection process using a Reduced Robust Random Cut Forest (RRRCF) data structure, which can be used on both small and large data sets. Similar to the Robust Random Cut Forest (RRCF), RRRCF is a structured, but a reduced representation of the training data sub-space in form of cut trees. Empirical results of this method on both low and high-dimensional data showed that inference about data being in/out of training distribution can be made efficiently and the model is easy to train with no difficult hyper-parameter tuning. The paper discusses two different use-cases for testing and validating results.
abstract_zh: 大多数基于机器学习的回归器从过去有限长度的观察收集的数据中提取信息，以对未来进行预测。因此，当输入到这些训练模型的数据与用于训练的数据具有显着不同的统计属性时，无法保证准确的预测。因此，在分布外的输入数据上使用这些模型可能会导致与期望结果完全不同的预测结果，这不仅是错误的，而且在某些情况下还可能是危险的。在任何系统中成功部署这些机器学习模型都需要一个检测系统，该系统应该能够区分分布外和分布内数据（即类似于训练数据）。在本文中，我们介绍了一种使用简化鲁棒随机森林（RRRCF）数据结构进行检测过程的新方法，该方法可用于小型和大型数据集。与鲁棒随机切割森林（RRCF）类似，RRCF 是一种结构化的、但以切割树形式减少的训练数据子空间的表示。该方法在低维和高维数据上的实证结果表明，可以有效地推断数据是否处于训练分布中，并且模型易于训练，无需困难的超参数调整。本文讨论了用于测试和验证结果的两种不同用例。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于缩减鲁棒随机切割森林（RRRCF）的数据结构，用于检测机器学习模型的输入数据是否超出训练数据的分布范围。
AI.motivation: 当输入数据的统计特性与训练数据显著不同时，机器学习模型的预测可能不准确，甚至带来危险。因此，需要一种有效的系统来检测输入数据是否超出训练分布。
AI.method: 本文引入了一种新的检测方法，使用缩减鲁棒随机切割森林（RRRCF）结构，该结构可以用于小规模和大规模数据集，并通过切割树的形式表示训练数据子空间。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在低维和高维数据上都能高效判断数据是否属于训练分布，并且模型易于训练，无需复杂的超参数调优。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法在检测输入数据是否超出训练分布方面表现良好，适用于多种应用场景，并具有良好的可扩展性和实用性。
