search_text: ReViSe: Remote Vital Signs Measurement Using Smartphone Camera ReViSe：使用智能手机摄像头进行远程生命体征测量 We propose an end-to-end framework to measure people's vital signs including Heart Rate (HR), Heart Rate Variability (HRV), Oxygen Saturation (SpO2) and Blood Pressure (BP) based on the rPPG methodology from the video of a user's face captured with a smartphone camera. We extract face landmarks with a deep learning-based neural network model in real-time. Multiple face patches also called Regions-of-Interest (RoIs) are extracted by using the predicted face landmarks. Several filters are applied to reduce the noise from the RoIs in the extracted cardiac signals called Blood Volume Pulse (BVP) signal. The measurements of HR, HRV and SpO2 are validated on two public rPPG datasets namely the TokyoTech rPPG and the Pulse Rate Detection (PURE) datasets, on which our models achieved the following Mean Absolute Errors (MAE): a) for HR, 1.73Beats-Per-Minute (bpm) and 3.95bpm respectively; b) for HRV, 18.55ms and 25.03ms respectively, and c) for SpO2, an MAE of 1.64% on the PURE dataset. We validated our end-to-end rPPG framework, ReViSe, in daily living environment, and thereby created the Video-HR dataset. Our HR estimation model achieved an MAE of 2.49bpm on this dataset. Since no publicly available rPPG datasets existed for BP measurement with face videos, we used a dataset with signals from fingertip sensor to train our deep learning-based BP estimation model and also created our own video dataset, Video-BP. On our Video-BP dataset, our BP estimation model achieved an MAE of 6.7mmHg for Systolic Blood Pressure (SBP), and an MAE of 9.6mmHg for Diastolic Blood Pressure (DBP). ReViSe framework has been validated on datasets with videos recorded in daily living environment as opposed to less noisy laboratory environment as reported by most state-of-the-art techniques. 我们提出了一个端到端框架来测量人们的生命体征，包括心率 (HR)、心率变异性 (HRV)、血氧饱和度 (SpO2) 和血压 (BP)，该框架基于使用智能手机摄像头捕获的用户面部视频的 rPPG 方法。我们使用基于深度学习的神经网络模型实时提取人脸特征点。通过使用预测的面部标志来提取也称为感兴趣区域 (RoI) 的多个面部补丁。应用多个滤波器来减少提取的心脏信号（称为血量脉冲 (BVP) 信号）中 RoI 的噪声。 HR、HRV 和 SpO2 的测量结果在两个公共 rPPG 数据集（即 TokyoTech rPPG 和脉搏率检测 (PURE) 数据集）上进行了验证，我们的模型在这两个数据集上实现了以下平均绝对误差 (MAE)：a) 对于 HR，分别为 1.73 次每分钟心跳 (bpm) 和 3.95bpm； b) 对于 HRV，分别为 18.55 毫秒和 25.03 毫秒，c) 对于 SpO2，PURE 数据集上的 MAE 为 1.64%。我们在日常生活环境中验证了我们的端到端 rPPG 框架 ReViSe，从而创建了 Video-HR 数据集。我们的 HR 估计模型在此数据集上实现了 2.49bpm 的 MAE。由于没有公开可用的 rPPG 数据集用于面部视频血压测量，因此我们使用包含指尖传感器信号的数据集来训练我们基于深度学习的血压估计模型，并创建了我们自己的视频数据集 Video-BP。在我们的 Video-BP 数据集上，我们的血压估计模型实现了收缩压 (SBP) 的 MAE 为 6.7mmHg，舒张压 (DBP) 的 MAE 为 9.6mmHg。 ReViSe 框架已在日常生活环境中录制的视频数据集上进行了验证，而不是大多数最先进技术报告的噪音较小的实验室环境。 本文提出ReViSe框架，利用智能手机摄像头通过远程光电容积描记法（rPPG）测量人体心率、心率变异性、血氧饱和度和血压。 Donghao Qiao,Amtul Haq Ayesha,Farhana Zulkernine,Raihan Masroor,Nauman Jaffar cs.CV,cs.HC,cs.LG,eess.IV,eess.SP

authors_text: Changdae Oh,Heeji Won,Junhyuk So,Taero Kim,Yewon Kim,Hosik Choi,Kyungwoo Song
title: Learning Fair Representation via Distributional Contrastive Disentanglement
title_zh: 通过分布对比解开学习公平表示
abstract: Learning fair representation is crucial for achieving fairness or debiasing sensitive information. Most existing works rely on adversarial representation learning to inject some invariance into representation. However, adversarial learning methods are known to suffer from relatively unstable training, and this might harm the balance between fairness and predictiveness of representation. We propose a new approach, learning FAir Representation via distributional CONtrastive Variational AutoEncoder (FarconVAE), which induces the latent space to be disentangled into sensitive and nonsensitive parts. We first construct the pair of observations with different sensitive attributes but with the same labels. Then, FarconVAE enforces each non-sensitive latent to be closer, while sensitive latents to be far from each other and also far from the non-sensitive latent by contrasting their distributions. We provide a new type of contrastive loss motivated by Gaussian and Student-t kernels for distributional contrastive learning with theoretical analysis. Besides, we adopt a new swap-reconstruction loss to boost the disentanglement further. FarconVAE shows superior performance on fairness, pretrained model debiasing, and domain generalization tasks from various modalities, including tabular, image, and text.
summary: Learning fair representation is crucial for achieving fairness or debiasing sensitive information. Most existing works rely on adversarial representation learning to inject some invariance into representation. However, adversarial learning methods are known to suffer from relatively unstable training, and this might harm the balance between fairness and predictiveness of representation. We propose a new approach, learning FAir Representation via distributional CONtrastive Variational AutoEncoder (FarconVAE), which induces the latent space to be disentangled into sensitive and nonsensitive parts. We first construct the pair of observations with different sensitive attributes but with the same labels. Then, FarconVAE enforces each non-sensitive latent to be closer, while sensitive latents to be far from each other and also far from the non-sensitive latent by contrasting their distributions. We provide a new type of contrastive loss motivated by Gaussian and Student-t kernels for distributional contrastive learning with theoretical analysis. Besides, we adopt a new swap-reconstruction loss to boost the disentanglement further. FarconVAE shows superior performance on fairness, pretrained model debiasing, and domain generalization tasks from various modalities, including tabular, image, and text.
abstract_zh: 学习公平表示对于实现公平或消除敏感信息的偏见至关重要。大多数现有的工作依赖于对抗性表示学习来为表示注入一些不变性。然而，众所周知，对抗性学习方法的训练相对不稳定，这可能会损害表示的公平性和预测性之间的平衡。我们提出了一种新方法，通过分布式对比变分自动编码器（FarconVAE）学习公平表示，这使得潜在空间被分解为敏感和非敏感部分。我们首先构建具有不同敏感属性但具有相同标签的观察对。然后，FarconVAE 通过对比它们的分布，强制每个非敏感潜在变量更接近，而敏感潜在变量彼此远离，并且也远离非敏感潜在变量。我们提供了一种由高斯核和 Student-t 核驱动的新型对比损失，用于通过理论分析进行分布式对比学习。此外，我们采用新的互换重建损失来进一步促进解缠结。 FarconVAE 在公平性、预训练模型去偏以及来自各种模式（包括表格、图像和文本）的领域泛化任务上表现出卓越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过分布对比解耦学习公平表示的新方法FarconVAE，解决传统对抗方法训练不稳定的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗性公平表示方法存在训练不稳定、难以平衡公平性与预测性的问题，需探索更稳定的解耦学习框架。
AI.method: 构建敏感属性不同但标签相同的样本对，通过对比损失使非敏感潜在变量趋近、敏感潜在变量远离，并引入交换重建损失增强解耦效果。
AI.result: 在表格、图像、文本等多模态数据上，FarconVAE在公平性、预训练模型去偏和领域泛化任务中均优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 分布对比解耦框架有效提升了公平表示学习的稳定性与性能，为公平AI提供了新思路。

authors_text: Daniel Honerkamp,Tim Welschehold,Abhinav Valada
title: N$^2$M$^2$: Learning Navigation for Arbitrary Mobile Manipulation Motions in Unseen and Dynamic Environments
title_zh: N$^2$M$^2$：学习在不可见和动态环境中任意移动操纵运动的导航
comment: Project website: http://mobile-rl.cs.uni-freiburg.de; Accepted at T-RO
comments: Project website: http://mobile-rl.cs.uni-freiburg.de; Accepted at T-RO
abstract: Despite its importance in both industrial and service robotics, mobile manipulation remains a significant challenge as it requires a seamless integration of end-effector trajectory generation with navigation skills as well as reasoning over long-horizons. Existing methods struggle to control the large configuration space, and to navigate dynamic and unknown environments. In previous work, we proposed to decompose mobile manipulation tasks into a simplified motion generator for the end-effector in task space and a trained reinforcement learning agent for the mobile base to account for kinematic feasibility of the motion. In this work, we introduce Neural Navigation for Mobile Manipulation (N$^2$M$^2$) which extends this decomposition to complex obstacle environments and enables it to tackle a broad range of tasks in real world settings. The resulting approach can perform unseen, long-horizon tasks in unexplored environments while instantly reacting to dynamic obstacles and environmental changes. At the same time, it provides a simple way to define new mobile manipulation tasks. We demonstrate the capabilities of our proposed approach in extensive simulation and real-world experiments on multiple kinematically diverse mobile manipulators. Code and videos are publicly available at http://mobile-rl.cs.uni-freiburg.de.
summary: Despite its importance in both industrial and service robotics, mobile manipulation remains a significant challenge as it requires a seamless integration of end-effector trajectory generation with navigation skills as well as reasoning over long-horizons. Existing methods struggle to control the large configuration space, and to navigate dynamic and unknown environments. In previous work, we proposed to decompose mobile manipulation tasks into a simplified motion generator for the end-effector in task space and a trained reinforcement learning agent for the mobile base to account for kinematic feasibility of the motion. In this work, we introduce Neural Navigation for Mobile Manipulation (N$^2$M$^2$) which extends this decomposition to complex obstacle environments and enables it to tackle a broad range of tasks in real world settings. The resulting approach can perform unseen, long-horizon tasks in unexplored environments while instantly reacting to dynamic obstacles and environmental changes. At the same time, it provides a simple way to define new mobile manipulation tasks. We demonstrate the capabilities of our proposed approach in extensive simulation and real-world experiments on multiple kinematically diverse mobile manipulators. Code and videos are publicly available at http://mobile-rl.cs.uni-freiburg.de.
abstract_zh: 尽管移动操纵在工业和服务机器人领域都很重要，但它仍然是一个重大挑战，因为它需要将末端执行器轨迹生成与导航技能以及长视野推理无缝集成。现有的方法很难控制大的配置空间，并难以驾驭动态和未知的环境。在之前的工作中，我们建议将移动操纵任务分解为任务空间中末端执行器的简化运动生成器和移动基座的经过训练的强化学习代理，以考虑运动的运动学可行性。在这项工作中，我们引入了移动操纵神经导航 (N$^2$M$^2$)，它将这种分解扩展到复杂的障碍环境，并使其能够处理现实世界环境中的广泛任务。由此产生的方法可以在未探索的环境中执行看不见的长期任务，同时对动态障碍和环境变化立即做出反应。同时，它提供了一种简单的方法来定义新的移动操作任务。我们在多个运动学不同的移动机械臂上进行广泛的模拟和现实实验，展示了我们提出的方法的功能。代码和视频可在 http://mobile-rl.cs.uni-freiburg.de 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出N²M²方法，解决移动机械臂在未知动态环境中执行复杂任务的导航与操作集成问题。
AI.motivation: 移动操作需要同时处理末端轨迹生成与导航，现有方法难以应对高维配置空间和动态未知环境的挑战。
AI.method: 将任务分解为任务空间的简化运动生成器与移动底盘的强化学习代理，扩展至复杂障碍物环境并支持动态反应。
AI.result: 在多种动力学不同的移动机械臂上通过仿真与实物实验验证，可执行未见过的长时序任务并适应环境变化。
AI.conclusion: N²M²通过模块化设计有效解决了移动操作的复杂性，为现实场景中的广泛任务提供了简单定义方式。

search_text: N$^2$M$^2$: Learning Navigation for Arbitrary Mobile Manipulation Motions in Unseen and Dynamic Environments N$^2$M$^2$：学习在不可见和动态环境中任意移动操纵运动的导航 Despite its importance in both industrial and service robotics, mobile manipulation remains a significant challenge as it requires a seamless integration of end-effector trajectory generation with navigation skills as well as reasoning over long-horizons. Existing methods struggle to control the large configuration space, and to navigate dynamic and unknown environments. In previous work, we proposed to decompose mobile manipulation tasks into a simplified motion generator for the end-effector in task space and a trained reinforcement learning agent for the mobile base to account for kinematic feasibility of the motion. In this work, we introduce Neural Navigation for Mobile Manipulation (N$^2$M$^2$) which extends this decomposition to complex obstacle environments and enables it to tackle a broad range of tasks in real world settings. The resulting approach can perform unseen, long-horizon tasks in unexplored environments while instantly reacting to dynamic obstacles and environmental changes. At the same time, it provides a simple way to define new mobile manipulation tasks. We demonstrate the capabilities of our proposed approach in extensive simulation and real-world experiments on multiple kinematically diverse mobile manipulators. Code and videos are publicly available at http://mobile-rl.cs.uni-freiburg.de. 尽管移动操纵在工业和服务机器人领域都很重要，但它仍然是一个重大挑战，因为它需要将末端执行器轨迹生成与导航技能以及长视野推理无缝集成。现有的方法很难控制大的配置空间，并难以驾驭动态和未知的环境。在之前的工作中，我们建议将移动操纵任务分解为任务空间中末端执行器的简化运动生成器和移动基座的经过训练的强化学习代理，以考虑运动的运动学可行性。在这项工作中，我们引入了移动操纵神经导航 (N$^2$M$^2$)，它将这种分解扩展到复杂的障碍环境，并使其能够处理现实世界环境中的广泛任务。由此产生的方法可以在未探索的环境中执行看不见的长期任务，同时对动态障碍和环境变化立即做出反应。同时，它提供了一种简单的方法来定义新的移动操作任务。我们在多个运动学不同的移动机械臂上进行广泛的模拟和现实实验，展示了我们提出的方法的功能。代码和视频可在 http://mobile-rl.cs.uni-freiburg.de 上公开获取。 本文提出N²M²方法，解决移动机械臂在未知动态环境中执行复杂任务的导航与操作集成问题。 Daniel Honerkamp,Tim Welschehold,Abhinav Valada cs.RO,cs.AI

authors_text: Wilfried Haensch,Anand Raghunathan,Kaushik Roy,Bhaswar Chakrabarti,Charudatta M. Phatak,Cheng Wang,Supratik Guha
title: A Co-design view of Compute in-Memory with Non-Volatile Elements for Neural Networks
title_zh: 神经网络内存计算与非易失性元素的协同​​设计视图
abstract: Deep Learning neural networks are pervasive, but traditional computer architectures are reaching the limits of being able to efficiently execute them for the large workloads of today. They are limited by the von Neumann bottleneck: the high cost in energy and latency incurred in moving data between memory and the compute engine. Today, special CMOS designs address this bottleneck. The next generation of computing hardware will need to eliminate or dramatically mitigate this bottleneck. We discuss how compute-in-memory can play an important part in this development. Here, a non-volatile memory based cross-bar architecture forms the heart of an engine that uses an analog process to parallelize the matrix vector multiplication operation, repeatedly used in all neural network workloads. The cross-bar architecture, at times referred to as a neuromorphic approach, can be a key hardware element in future computing machines. In the first part of this review we take a co-design view of the design constraints and the demands it places on the new materials and memory devices that anchor the cross-bar architecture. In the second part, we review what is knows about the different new non-volatile memory materials and devices suited for compute in-memory, and discuss the outlook and challenges.
summary: Deep Learning neural networks are pervasive, but traditional computer architectures are reaching the limits of being able to efficiently execute them for the large workloads of today. They are limited by the von Neumann bottleneck: the high cost in energy and latency incurred in moving data between memory and the compute engine. Today, special CMOS designs address this bottleneck. The next generation of computing hardware will need to eliminate or dramatically mitigate this bottleneck. We discuss how compute-in-memory can play an important part in this development. Here, a non-volatile memory based cross-bar architecture forms the heart of an engine that uses an analog process to parallelize the matrix vector multiplication operation, repeatedly used in all neural network workloads. The cross-bar architecture, at times referred to as a neuromorphic approach, can be a key hardware element in future computing machines. In the first part of this review we take a co-design view of the design constraints and the demands it places on the new materials and memory devices that anchor the cross-bar architecture. In the second part, we review what is knows about the different new non-volatile memory materials and devices suited for compute in-memory, and discuss the outlook and challenges.
abstract_zh: 深度学习神经网络很普遍，但传统的计算机架构已经达到了能够有效执行当今大量工作负载的极限。它们受到冯诺依曼瓶颈的限制：在内存和计算引擎之间移动数据时会产生高昂的能源成本和延迟。如今，特殊的 CMOS 设计解决了这一瓶颈。下一代计算硬件将需要消除或显着缓解这一瓶颈。我们讨论内存计算如何在这一发展中发挥重要作用。这里，基于非易失性存储器的交叉架构构成了引擎的核心，该引擎使用模拟过程来并行化矩阵向量乘法运算，并在所有神经网络工作负载中重复使用。交叉架构有时被称为神经形态方法，可以成为未来计算机器的关键硬件元素。在本次审查的第一部分中，我们对设计约束及其对锚定交叉架构的新材料和存储设备的要求进行了协同设计。在第二部分中，我们回顾了对适合内存计算的不同新型非易失性存储材料和设备的了解，并讨论了前景和挑战。

AI.tldr: 本文从共设计视角探讨基于非易失性元件的存算一体架构在神经网络中的应用，以解决传统计算机架构的冯·诺依曼瓶颈问题。
AI.motivation: 传统计算机架构因冯·诺依曼瓶颈导致数据搬运能耗与延迟过高，难以高效处理大规模深度学习任务，亟需新型计算硬件方案。
AI.method: 采用共设计方法，分析存算一体架构对新材料和器件的需求，并综述适用于存算一体的非易失性存储技术及其挑战。
AI.result: 非易失性交叉条架构可通过模拟计算并行化矩阵向量乘法，成为未来计算设备的关键硬件，但材料与设计约束仍需突破。
AI.conclusion: 存算一体架构是突破冯·诺依曼瓶颈的有效方向，但需在材料创新与共设计方法上进一步研究以实现实际应用。

authors_text: Afonso S. Bandeira,Antoine Maillard,Nikita Zhivotovskiy
title: A remark on Kashin's discrepancy argument and partial coloring in the Komlós conjecture
title_zh: 关于科姆洛斯猜想中卡申不符论证和部分着色的评述
abstract: In this expository note, we discuss an early partial coloring result of B. Kashin [C. R. Acad. Bulgare Sci., 1985]. Although this result only implies Spencer's six standard deviations [Trans. Amer. Math. Soc., 1985] up to a $\log\log n$ factor, Kashin's argument gives a simple proof of the existence of a constant discrepancy partial coloring in the setup of Komlós conjecture.
summary: In this expository note, we discuss an early partial coloring result of B. Kashin [C. R. Acad. Bulgare Sci., 1985]. Although this result only implies Spencer's six standard deviations [Trans. Amer. Math. Soc., 1985] up to a $\log\log n$ factor, Kashin's argument gives a simple proof of the existence of a constant discrepancy partial coloring in the setup of Komlós conjecture.
abstract_zh: 在本说明性注释中，我们讨论 B. Kashin [C. R.阿卡德。保加利亚科学，1985]。尽管这个结果只暗示了斯宾塞的六个标准差[Trans。阿米尔。数学。 Soc., 1985] 直到 $\log\log n$ 因子，Kashin 的论证给出了在 Komlós 猜想的设置中存在恒定差异部分着色的简单证明。

AI.tldr: 讨论Kashin的部分着色结果及其在Komlós猜想中的应用
AI.motivation: 探讨Komlós猜想中部分着色的常数差异性存在性，并简化相关证明
AI.method: 通过分析Kashin的差异性论证，提供一个简化的证明方法
AI.result: 证明了Komlós猜想中存在常数差异性的部分着色，尽管与Spencer结果存在log log n因子差异
AI.conclusion: Kashin的方法为Komlós猜想提供了简洁的证明，突显其在差异性理论中的重要性

Soc., 1985] up to a $\log\log n$ factor, Kashin's argument gives a simple proof of the existence of a constant discrepancy partial coloring in the setup of Komlós conjecture. 在本说明性注释中，我们讨论 B. Kashin [C. R.阿卡德。保加利亚科学，1985]。尽管这个结果只暗示了斯宾塞的六个标准差[Trans。阿米尔。数学。 Soc., 1985] 直到 $\log\log n$ 因子，Kashin 的论证给出了在 Komlós 猜想的设置中存在恒定差异部分着色的简单证明。 讨论Kashin的部分着色结果及其在Komlós猜想中的应用 Afonso S. Bandeira,Antoine Maillard,Nikita Zhivotovskiy math.CO,cs.DM,cs.DS,math.MG

authors_text: Khairuldanial Ismail,Ran Liu,Zhenghong Qin,Achala Athukorala,Billy Pik Lik Lau,Muhammad Shalihan,Chau Yuen,U-Xuan Tan
title: Efficient WiFi LiDAR SLAM for Autonomous Robots in Large Environments
title_zh: 适用于大型环境中自主机器人的高效 WiFi LiDAR SLAM
comment: accepted by the 2022 IEEE 18th International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE)
comments: accepted by the 2022 IEEE 18th International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE)
abstract: Autonomous robots operating in indoor and GPS denied environments can use LiDAR for SLAM instead. However, LiDARs do not perform well in geometrically-degraded environments, due to the challenge of loop closure detection and computational load to perform scan matching. Existing WiFi infrastructure can be exploited for localization and mapping with low hardware and computational cost. Yet, accurate pose estimation using WiFi is challenging as different signal values can be measured at the same location due to the unpredictability of signal propagation. Therefore, we introduce the use of WiFi fingerprint sequence for pose estimation (i.e. loop closure) in SLAM. This approach exploits the spatial coherence of location fingerprints obtained while a mobile robot is moving. This has better capability of correcting odometry drift. The method also incorporates LiDAR scans and thus, improving computational efficiency for large and geometrically-degraded environments while maintaining the accuracy of LiDAR SLAM. We conducted experiments in an indoor environment to illustrate the effectiveness of the method. The results are evaluated based on Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and it has achieved an accuracy of 0.88m for the test environment.
summary: Autonomous robots operating in indoor and GPS denied environments can use LiDAR for SLAM instead. However, LiDARs do not perform well in geometrically-degraded environments, due to the challenge of loop closure detection and computational load to perform scan matching. Existing WiFi infrastructure can be exploited for localization and mapping with low hardware and computational cost. Yet, accurate pose estimation using WiFi is challenging as different signal values can be measured at the same location due to the unpredictability of signal propagation. Therefore, we introduce the use of WiFi fingerprint sequence for pose estimation (i.e. loop closure) in SLAM. This approach exploits the spatial coherence of location fingerprints obtained while a mobile robot is moving. This has better capability of correcting odometry drift. The method also incorporates LiDAR scans and thus, improving computational efficiency for large and geometrically-degraded environments while maintaining the accuracy of LiDAR SLAM. We conducted experiments in an indoor environment to illustrate the effectiveness of the method. The results are evaluated based on Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and it has achieved an accuracy of 0.88m for the test environment.
abstract_zh: 在室内和 GPS 无法使用的环境中运行的自主机器人可以使用 LiDAR 进行 SLAM。然而，由于闭环检测和执行扫描匹配的计算负载的挑战，激光雷达在几何退化的环境中表现不佳。现有的 WiFi 基础设施可用于定位和绘图，且硬件和计算成本较低。然而，使用 WiFi 进行准确的姿态估计具有挑战性，因为由于信号传播的不可预测性，可以在同一位置测量不同的信号值。因此，我们引入了在 SLAM 中使用 WiFi 指纹序列进行位姿估计（即回环）。这种方法利用了移动机器人移动时获得的位置指纹的空间一致性。这具有更好的修正里程漂移的能力。该方法还结合了 LiDAR 扫描，从而提高了大型和几何退化环境的计算效率，同时保持了 LiDAR SLAM 的准确性。我们在室内环境中进行了实验来说明该方法的有效性。结果基于均方根误差（RMSE）进行评估，在测试环境下达到了0.88m的精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合WiFi指纹序列与LiDAR数据的高效SLAM方法，用于改善自主机器人在大范围及几何退化环境中的定位与建图性能。
AI.motivation: 传统LiDAR SLAM在几何退化环境中存在回环检测困难和计算负载高的问题，而WiFi定位虽成本低但存在信号不确定性导致精度不足，需融合二者优势以提升性能。
AI.method: 利用移动机器人运动过程中获取的WiFi指纹空间一致性进行位姿估计（回环检测），并结合LiDAR扫描数据，降低计算复杂度同时保持定位精度。
AI.result: 在室内环境中实验验证，该方法的定位误差（RMSE）达到0.88米，有效纠正了里程计漂移并提升了大范围环境下的SLAM效率。
AI.conclusion: 通过WiFi指纹与LiDAR的协同，该方法在保持LiDAR SLAM精度的同时，显著优化了复杂环境下的计算效率与定位鲁棒性。

authors_text: Roman Novak,Jascha Sohl-Dickstein,Samuel S. Schoenholz
title: Fast Finite Width Neural Tangent Kernel
title_zh: 快速有限宽度神经正切核
comment: Published as a conference paper at ICML 2022
comments: Published as a conference paper at ICML 2022
abstract: The Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK), defined as $Θ_θ^f(x_1, x_2) = \left[\partial f(θ, x_1)\big/\partial θ\right] \left[\partial f(θ, x_2)\big/\partial θ\right]^T$ where $\left[\partial f(θ, \cdot)\big/\partial θ\right]$ is a neural network (NN) Jacobian, has emerged as a central object of study in deep learning. In the infinite width limit, the NTK can sometimes be computed analytically and is useful for understanding training and generalization of NN architectures. At finite widths, the NTK is also used to better initialize NNs, compare the conditioning across models, perform architecture search, and do meta-learning. Unfortunately, the finite width NTK is notoriously expensive to compute, which severely limits its practical utility. We perform the first in-depth analysis of the compute and memory requirements for NTK computation in finite width networks. Leveraging the structure of neural networks, we further propose two novel algorithms that change the exponent of the compute and memory requirements of the finite width NTK, dramatically improving efficiency. Our algorithms can be applied in a black box fashion to any differentiable function, including those implementing neural networks. We open-source our implementations within the Neural Tangents package (arXiv:1912.02803) at https://github.com/google/neural-tangents.
summary: The Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK), defined as $Θ_θ^f(x_1, x_2) = \left[\partial f(θ, x_1)\big/\partial θ\right] \left[\partial f(θ, x_2)\big/\partial θ\right]^T$ where $\left[\partial f(θ, \cdot)\big/\partial θ\right]$ is a neural network (NN) Jacobian, has emerged as a central object of study in deep learning. In the infinite width limit, the NTK can sometimes be computed analytically and is useful for understanding training and generalization of NN architectures. At finite widths, the NTK is also used to better initialize NNs, compare the conditioning across models, perform architecture search, and do meta-learning. Unfortunately, the finite width NTK is notoriously expensive to compute, which severely limits its practical utility. We perform the first in-depth analysis of the compute and memory requirements for NTK computation in finite width networks. Leveraging the structure of neural networks, we further propose two novel algorithms that change the exponent of the compute and memory requirements of the finite width NTK, dramatically improving efficiency. Our algorithms can be applied in a black box fashion to any differentiable function, including those implementing neural networks. We open-source our implementations within the Neural Tangents package (arXiv:1912.02803) at https://github.com/google/neural-tangents.
abstract_zh: 神经正切核 (NTK)，定义为 $θ_θ^f(x_1, x_2) = \left[\partial f(θ, x_1)\big/\partial θ\right] \left[\partial f(θ, x_2)\big/\partial θ\right]^T$ 其中 $\left[\partial f(θ, \cdot)\big/\partial θ\right]$ 是一个神经网络(NN) 雅可比行列式已成为深度学习的中心研究对象。在无限宽度限制下，NTK 有时可以进行分析计算，对于理解神经网络架构的训练和泛化很有用。在有限宽度下，NTK 还用于更好地初始化神经网络、比较模型之间的条件、执行架构搜索和进行元学习。不幸的是，众所周知，有限宽度 NTK 的计算成本非常昂贵，这严重限制了它的实际用途。我们对有限宽度网络中 NTK 计算的计算和内存要求进行了首次深入分析。利用神经网络的结构，我们进一步提出了两种新颖的算法，可以改变有限宽度 NTK 的计算和内存需求的指数，从而显着提高效率。我们的算法可以以黑盒方式应用于任何可微函数，包括那些实现神经网络的函数。我们在 Neural Tangents 包 (arXiv:1912.02803) 中开源了我们的实现，网址为 https://github.com/google/neural-tangents。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在分析有限宽度神经切线核（NTK）的计算与内存需求，并提出两种高效算法以提升其计算效率。
AI.motivation: 尽管NTK在无限宽度极限下具有理论价值，但有限宽度下的NTK计算成本高昂，限制了其在实际应用中的效用。
AI.method: 通过分析神经网络结构，提出两种新型算法，降低有限宽度NTK的计算与内存复杂度。
AI.result: 算法显著提升NTK计算效率，适用于任意可微函数，并开源实现于Neural Tangents库。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法有效解决有限宽度NTK的计算瓶颈，推动其在模型初始化、架构搜索等任务中的应用。

authors_text: Yijun Yuan,Andreas Nuechter
title: An Algorithm for the SE(3)-Transformation on Neural Implicit Maps for Remapping Functions
title_zh: 用于重映射函数的神经隐式映射的SE(3)变换算法
comment: Accepted to RAL2022, code at https://github.com/Jarrome/IMT_Mapping
comments: Accepted to RAL2022, code at https://github.com/Jarrome/IMT_Mapping
abstract: Implicit representations are widely used for object reconstruction due to their efficiency and flexibility. In 2021, a novel structure named neural implicit map has been invented for incremental reconstruction. A neural implicit map alleviates the problem of inefficient memory cost of previous online 3D dense reconstruction while producing better quality. % However, the neural implicit map suffers the limitation that it does not support remapping as the frames of scans are encoded into a deep prior after generating the neural implicit map. This means, that neither this generation process is invertible, nor a deep prior is transformable. The non-remappable property makes it not possible to apply loop-closure techniques. % We present a neural implicit map based transformation algorithm to fill this gap. As our neural implicit map is transformable, our model supports remapping for this special map of latent features. % Experiments show that our remapping module is capable to well-transform neural implicit maps to new poses. Embedded into a SLAM framework, our mapping model is able to tackle the remapping of loop closures and demonstrates high-quality surface reconstruction. % Our implementation is available at github\footnote{\url{https://github.com/Jarrome/IMT_Mapping}} for the research community.

As our neural implicit map is transformable, our model supports remapping for this special map of latent features. % Experiments show that our remapping module is capable to well-transform neural implicit maps to new poses. Embedded into a SLAM framework, our mapping model is able to tackle the remapping of loop closures and demonstrates high-quality surface reconstruction. % Our implementation is available at github\footnote{\url{https://github.com/Jarrome/IMT_Mapping}} for the research community.
abstract_zh: 隐式表示由于其效率和灵活性而被广泛用于对象重建。 2021年，一种名为神经隐式映射的新颖结构被发明，用于增量重建。神经隐式映射缓解了先前在线 3D 密集重建的低效内存成本问题，同时产生了更好的质量。然而，神经隐式映射受到限制，即它不支持重新映射，因为在生成神经隐式映射后，扫描帧被编码到深度先验中。这意味着，这个生成过程不是可逆的，深度先验也不是可转换的。不可重映射的属性使得不可能应用循环闭合技术。 ％我们提出了一种基于神经隐式映射的变换算法来填补这一空白。由于我们的神经隐式映射是可变换的，因此我们的模型支持对这种特殊的潜在特征映射进行重新映射。 % 实验表明，我们的重新映射模块能够很好地将神经隐式映射转换为新姿势。我们的映射模型嵌入到 SLAM 框架中，能够处理闭环的重新映射并演示高质量的表面重建。 % 研究社区可以在 github\footnote{\url{https://github.com/Jarrome/IMT_Mapping}} 上找到我们的实现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对神经隐式映射的SE(3)变换算法，以解决其不可重映射的问题。
AI.motivation: 神经隐式映射虽在增量重建中高效灵活，但生成后无法重映射，限制了其在SLAM闭环检测中的应用。
AI.method: 设计可变换的神经隐式映射结构，并引入重映射模块实现潜特征映射的位姿变换。
AI.result: 实验表明该模块能有效变换神经隐式映射至新位姿，嵌入SLAM框架后可处理闭环重映射并实现高质量表面重建。
AI.conclusion: 本文方法填补了神经隐式映射不可重映射的空白，为增量重建与SLAM结合提供了新方案。

search_text: An Algorithm for the SE(3)-Transformation on Neural Implicit Maps for Remapping Functions 用于重映射函数的神经隐式映射的SE(3)变换算法 Implicit representations are widely used for object reconstruction due to their efficiency and flexibility. In 2021, a novel structure named neural implicit map has been invented for incremental reconstruction. A neural implicit map alleviates the problem of inefficient memory cost of previous online 3D dense reconstruction while producing better quality. % However, the neural implicit map suffers the limitation that it does not support remapping as the frames of scans are encoded into a deep prior after generating the neural implicit map. This means, that neither this generation process is invertible, nor a deep prior is transformable. The non-remappable property makes it not possible to apply loop-closure techniques. % We present a neural implicit map based transformation algorithm to fill this gap. As our neural implicit map is transformable, our model supports remapping for this special map of latent features. % Experiments show that our remapping module is capable to well-transform neural implicit maps to new poses. authors_text: Charles Wan,Rodrigo Belo,Leid Zejnilović
title: Explainability's Gain is Optimality's Loss? -- How Explanations Bias Decision-making
title_zh: 可解释性的增益是最优性的损失？ ——解释如何影响决策
comment: To appear in the Proceedings of the 2022 AAAI/ACM Conference on AI, Ethics, and Society (AIES '22)
comments: To appear in the Proceedings of the 2022 AAAI/ACM Conference on AI, Ethics, and Society (AIES '22)
abstract: Decisions in organizations are about evaluating alternatives and choosing the one that would best serve organizational goals. To the extent that the evaluation of alternatives could be formulated as a predictive task with appropriate metrics, machine learning algorithms are increasingly being used to improve the efficiency of the process. Explanations help to facilitate communication between the algorithm and the human decision-maker, making it easier for the latter to interpret and make decisions on the basis of predictions by the former. Feature-based explanations' semantics of causal models, however, induce leakage from the decision-maker's prior beliefs. Our findings from a field experiment demonstrate empirically how this leads to confirmation bias and disparate impact on the decision-maker's confidence in the predictions. Such differences can lead to sub-optimal and biased decision outcomes.
summary: Decisions in organizations are about evaluating alternatives and choosing the one that would best serve organizational goals. To the extent that the evaluation of alternatives could be formulated as a predictive task with appropriate metrics, machine learning algorithms are increasingly being used to improve the efficiency of the process. Explanations help to facilitate communication between the algorithm and the human decision-maker, making it easier for the latter to interpret and make decisions on the basis of predictions by the former. Feature-based explanations' semantics of causal models, however, induce leakage from the decision-maker's prior beliefs. Our findings from a field experiment demonstrate empirically how this leads to confirmation bias and disparate impact on the decision-maker's confidence in the predictions. Such differences can lead to sub-optimal and biased decision outcomes.
abstract_zh: 组织中的决策涉及评估替代方案并选择最能服务于组织目标的方案。在某种程度上，替代方案的评估可以被表述为具有适当指标的预测任务，机器学习算法越来越多地被用来提高流程的效率。解释有助于促进算法和人类决策者之间的沟通，使后者更容易根据前者的预测进行解释和决策。然而，基于特征的因果模型解释语义会导致决策者先前信念的泄漏。我们的现场实验结果凭经验证明了这如何导致确认偏差并对决策者对预测的信心产生不同的影响。这种差异可能会导致次优且有偏见的决策结果。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了可解释性AI在提升决策透明度的同时可能引入的偏差问题，揭示解释性与决策最优性之间的权衡关系。
AI.motivation: 组织决策依赖机器学习提高效率，但特征解释可能因引入决策者先验信念而产生偏差，影响决策质量。
AI.method: 通过实地实验验证基于因果模型的特征解释如何引发确认偏差及决策置信度差异。
AI.result: 实验表明特征解释导致确认偏差和不同影响，使决策者对预测的置信度产生差异，最终产生次优和有偏的决策结果。
AI.conclusion: 解释性AI的透明性可能以牺牲决策最优性为代价，需在解释性与决策质量间寻求平衡。

authors_text: Tejaswi Kasarla,Gertjan J. Burghouts,Max van Spengler,Elise van der Pol,Rita Cucchiara,Pascal Mettes
title: Maximum Class Separation as Inductive Bias in One Matrix
title_zh: 作为一个矩阵中的感应偏置的最大类分离
abstract: Maximizing the separation between classes constitutes a well-known inductive bias in machine learning and a pillar of many traditional algorithms. By default, deep networks are not equipped with this inductive bias and therefore many alternative solutions have been proposed through differential optimization. Current approaches tend to optimize classification and separation jointly: aligning inputs with class vectors and separating class vectors angularly. This paper proposes a simple alternative: encoding maximum separation as an inductive bias in the network by adding one fixed matrix multiplication before computing the softmax activations. The main observation behind our approach is that separation does not require optimization but can be solved in closed-form prior to training and plugged into a network. We outline a recursive approach to obtain the matrix consisting of maximally separable vectors for any number of classes, which can be added with negligible engineering effort and computational overhead. Despite its simple nature, this one matrix multiplication provides real impact. We show that our proposal directly boosts classification, long-tailed recognition, out-of-distribution detection, and open-set recognition, from CIFAR to ImageNet. We find empirically that maximum separation works best as a fixed bias; making the matrix learnable adds nothing to the performance. The closed-form implementation and code to reproduce the experiments are available on github.
summary: Maximizing the separation between classes constitutes a well-known inductive bias in machine learning and a pillar of many traditional algorithms. By default, deep networks are not equipped with this inductive bias and therefore many alternative solutions have been proposed through differential optimization. Current approaches tend to optimize classification and separation jointly: aligning inputs with class vectors and separating class vectors angularly. This paper proposes a simple alternative: encoding maximum separation as an inductive bias in the network by adding one fixed matrix multiplication before computing the softmax activations. The main observation behind our approach is that separation does not require optimization but can be solved in closed-form prior to training and plugged into a network. We outline a recursive approach to obtain the matrix consisting of maximally separable vectors for any number of classes, which can be added with negligible engineering effort and computational overhead. Despite its simple nature, this one matrix multiplication provides real impact. We show that our proposal directly boosts classification, long-tailed recognition, out-of-distribution detection, and open-set recognition, from CIFAR to ImageNet. We find empirically that maximum separation works best as a fixed bias; making the matrix learnable adds nothing to the performance. The closed-form implementation and code to reproduce the experiments are available on github.
abstract_zh: 最大化类之间的分离构成了机器学习中众所周知的归纳偏差，也是许多传统算法的支柱。默认情况下，深度网络不具备这种归纳偏差，因此通过差分优化提出了许多替代解决方案。当前的方法倾向于联合优化分类和分离：将输入与类向量对齐并按角度分离类向量。本文提出了一种简单的替代方案：通过在计算 softmax 激活之前添加一个固定矩阵乘法，将最大分离编码为网络中的归纳偏差。我们方法背后的主要观察是分离不需要优化，但可以在训练之前以封闭形式解决并插入网络。我们概述了一种递归方法来获取由任意数量的类的最大可分离向量组成的矩阵，该矩阵的添加可以忽略不计的工程工作和计算开销。尽管其本质很简单，但这一矩阵乘法却带来了真正的影响。我们表明，我们的提案直接提升了从 CIFAR 到 ImageNet 的分类、长尾识别、分布外检测和开放集识别。我们根据经验发现，最大分离作为固定偏差效果最好。使矩阵可学习不会对性能产生任何影响。封闭形式的实现和重现实验的代码可在 github 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过添加一个固定矩阵乘法作为归纳偏置，实现最大类别分离，从而提升深度学习模型的分类性能。
AI.motivation: 深度网络默认缺乏类别分离的归纳偏置，现有方法通过联合优化分类与分离实现目标，但过程复杂。本文旨在提供一种简单高效的替代方案。
AI.method: 在softmax激活前添加一个固定矩阵乘法，该矩阵通过闭式解递归生成，无需训练即可实现最大类别分离。
AI.result: 该方法在CIFAR到ImageNet等数据集上显著提升分类、长尾识别、分布外检测和开放集识别性能，且固定矩阵比可学习矩阵更优。
AI.conclusion: 最大分离作为固定归纳偏置能有效提升模型性能，其简单实现方式具有广泛适用性且无需额外计算开销。

result: 该方法在CIFAR到ImageNet等数据集上显著提升分类、长尾识别、分布外检测和开放集识别性能，且固定矩阵比可学习矩阵更优。
conclusion: 最大分离作为固定归纳偏置能有效提升模型性能，其简单实现方式具有广泛适用性且无需额外计算开销。
search_text: Maximum Class Separation as Inductive Bias in One Matrix 作为一个矩阵中的感应偏置的最大类分离 Maximizing the separation between classes constitutes a well-known inductive bias in machine learning and a pillar of many traditional algorithms. By default, deep networks are not equipped with this inductive bias and therefore many alternative solutions have been proposed through differential optimization. Current approaches tend to optimize classification and separation jointly: aligning inputs with class vectors and separating class vectors angularly. This paper proposes a simple alternative: encoding maximum separation as an inductive bias in the network by adding one fixed matrix multiplication before computing the softmax activations. The main observation behind our approach is that separation does not require optimization but can be solved in closed-form prior to training and plugged into a network. We outline a recursive approach to obtain the matrix consisting of maximally separable vectors for any number of classes, which can be added with negligible engineering effort and computational overhead. Despite its simple nature, this one matrix multiplication provides real impact. We show that our proposal directly boosts classification, long-tailed recognition, out-of-distribution detection, and open-set recognition, from CIFAR to ImageNet. We find empirically that maximum separation works best as a fixed bias; making the matrix learnable adds nothing to the performance. The closed-form implementation and code to reproduce the experiments are available on github. 最大化类之间的分离构成了机器学习中众所周知的归纳偏差，也是许多传统算法的支柱。默认情况下，深度网络不具备这种归纳偏差，因此通过差分优化提出了许多替代解决方案。当前的方法倾向于联合优化分类和分离：将输入与类向量对齐并按角度分离类向量。本文提出了一种简单的替代方案：通过在计算 softmax 激活之前添加一个固定矩阵乘法，将最大分离编码为网络中的归纳偏差。我们方法背后的主要观察是分离不需要优化，但可以在训练之前以封闭形式解决并插入网络。我们概述了一种递归方法来获取由任意数量的类的最大可分离向量组成的矩阵，该矩阵的添加可以忽略不计的工程工作和计算开销。尽管其本质很简单，但这一矩阵乘法却带来了真正的影响。我们表明，我们的提案直接提升了从 CIFAR 到 ImageNet 的分类、长尾识别、分布外检测和开放集识别。我们根据经验发现，最大分离作为固定偏差效果最好。使矩阵可学习不会对性能产生任何影响。封闭形式的实现和重现实验的代码可在 github 上找到。 本文提出通过添加一个固定矩阵乘法作为归纳偏置，实现最大类别分离，从而提升深度学习模型的分类性能。 Tejaswi Kasarla,Gertjan J. Burghouts,Max van Spengler,Elise van der Pol,Rita Cucchiara,Pascal Mettes cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Jonas Van Der Donckt,Jeroen Van Der Donckt,Emiel Deprost,Sofie Van Hoecke
title: Plotly-Resampler: Effective Visual Analytics for Large Time Series
title_zh: Plotly-Resampler：大型时间序列的有效可视化分析
comment: The first two authors contributed equally. Accepted at IEEE VIS 2022
comments: The first two authors contributed equally. Accepted at IEEE VIS 2022
abstract: Visual analytics is arguably the most important step in getting acquainted with your data. This is especially the case for time series, as this data type is hard to describe and cannot be fully understood when using for example summary statistics. To realize effective time series visualization, four requirements have to be met; a tool should be (1) interactive, (2) scalable to millions of data points, (3) integrable in conventional data science environments, and (4) highly configurable. We observe that open source Python visualization toolkits empower data scientists in most visual analytics tasks, but lack the combination of scalability and interactivity to realize effective time series visualization. As a means to facilitate these requirements, we created Plotly-Resampler, an open source Python library. Plotly-Resampler is an add-on for Plotly's Python bindings, enhancing line chart scalability on top of an interactive toolkit by aggregating the underlying data depending on the current graph view. Plotly-Resampler is built to be snappy, as the reactivity of a tool qualitatively affects how analysts visually explore and analyze data. A benchmark task highlights how our toolkit scales better than alternatives in terms of number of samples and time series. Additionally, Plotly-Resampler's flexible data aggregation functionality paves the path towards researching novel aggregation techniques. Plotly-Resampler's integrability, together with its configurability, convenience, and high scalability, allows to effectively analyze high-frequency data in your day-to-day Python environment.

A benchmark task highlights how our toolkit scales better than alternatives in terms of number of samples and time series. Additionally, Plotly-Resampler's flexible data aggregation functionality paves the path towards researching novel aggregation techniques. Plotly-Resampler's integrability, together with its configurability, convenience, and high scalability, allows to effectively analyze high-frequency data in your day-to-day Python environment.
abstract_zh: 可视化分析可以说是熟悉数据的最重要的一步。对于时间序列来说尤其如此，因为这种数据类型很难描述，并且在使用汇总统计等时无法完全理解。要实现有效的时间序列可视化，必须满足四个要求；工具应该是（1）交互式的，（2）可扩展至数百万个数据点，（3）可集成在传统的数据科学环境中，以及（4）高度可配置。我们观察到，开源 Python 可视化工具包使数据科学家能够完成大多数可视化分析任务，但缺乏可扩展性和交互性的结合来实现有效的时间序列可视化。为了满足这些要求，我们创建了 Plotly-Resampler，一个开源 Python 库。 Plotly-Resampler 是 Plotly 的 Python 绑定的附加组件，通过根据当前图形视图聚合底层数据来增强交互式工具包之上的折线图可扩展性。 Plotly-Resampler 的设计非常敏捷，因为工具的反应性会定性地影响分析师直观地探索和分析数据的方式。基准测试任务强调了我们的工具包如何在样本数量和时间序列方面比替代方案更好地扩展。此外，Plotly-Resampler 灵活的数据聚合功能为研究新颖的聚合技术铺平了道路。 Plotly-Resampler 的可集成性、可配置性、便利性和高可扩展性，使您能够在日常 Python 环境中有效地分析高频数据。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Plotly-Resampler，一个用于大规模时间序列数据的可视化分析工具，通过动态数据聚合实现交互性与可扩展性的结合。
AI.motivation: 现有开源可视化工具难以同时满足大规模时间序列数据的交互性与可扩展性需求，限制了有效数据分析。
AI.method: 开发Plotly-Resampler作为Plotly的扩展库，根据图表视图动态聚合数据，提升交互性能与数据处理规模。
AI.result: 基准测试显示其在数据量和时间序列处理上优于现有工具，且聚合功能支持新型分析方法研究。
AI.conclusion: Plotly-Resampler凭借高集成性、可配置性及扩展性，成为日常Python环境中分析高频数据的有效解决方案。

authors_text: Lin Li,Guoli Wang,Xuemei Guo
title: Towards Real-Time Visual Tracking with Graded Color-names Features
title_zh: 利用分级颜色名称功能实现实时视觉跟踪
abstract: MeanShift algorithm has been widely used in tracking tasks because of its simplicity and efficiency. However, the traditional MeanShift algorithm needs to label the initial region of the target, which reduces the applicability of the algorithm. Furthermore, it is only applicable to the scene with a large overlap rate between the target area and the candidate area. Therefore, when the target speed is fast, the target scale change, shape deformation or the target occlusion occurs, the tracking performance will be deteriorated. In this paper, we address the challenges above-mentioned by developing a tracking method that combines the background models and the graded features of color-names under the MeanShift framework. This method significantly improve performance in the above scenarios. In addition, it facilitates the balance between detection accuracy and detection speed. Experimental results demonstrate the validation of the proposed method.
summary: MeanShift algorithm has been widely used in tracking tasks because of its simplicity and efficiency. However, the traditional MeanShift algorithm needs to label the initial region of the target, which reduces the applicability of the algorithm. Furthermore, it is only applicable to the scene with a large overlap rate between the target area and the candidate area. Therefore, when the target speed is fast, the target scale change, shape deformation or the target occlusion occurs, the tracking performance will be deteriorated. In this paper, we address the challenges above-mentioned by developing a tracking method that combines the background models and the graded features of color-names under the MeanShift framework. This method significantly improve performance in the above scenarios. In addition, it facilitates the balance between detection accuracy and detection speed. Experimental results demonstrate the validation of the proposed method.
abstract_zh: MeanShift算法因其简单、高效而被广泛应用于跟踪任务中。然而传统的MeanShift算法需要对目标的初始区域进行标记，降低了算法的适用性。而且，仅适用于目标区域与候选区域重叠率较大的场景。因此，当目标速度较快、目标发生尺度变化、形状变形或目标遮挡时，跟踪性能将会变差。在本文中，我们通过开发一种在 MeanShift 框架下结合背景模型和颜色名称分级特征的跟踪方法来解决上述挑战。该方法显着提高了上述场景的性能。此外，有利于检测精度和检测速度之间的平衡。实验结果证明了所提出方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合背景模型与分级颜色名称特征的实时视觉跟踪方法，以解决传统MeanShift算法的局限性。
AI.motivation: 传统MeanShift算法需手动标记初始目标区域且对目标运动速度、形变及遮挡敏感，导致适用场景受限。
AI.method: 在MeanShift框架下融合背景模型与分级颜色名称特征，提升目标区域与候选区域的匹配鲁棒性并平衡检测速度与精度。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在目标快速运动、尺度变化及遮挡场景下显著优于传统算法，验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法有效缓解了传统MeanShift算法的缺陷，在复杂场景下实现了更稳定实时的视觉跟踪。

authors_text: Rafael Ballester-Ripoll,Manuele Leonelli
title: You Only Derive Once (YODO): Automatic Differentiation for Efficient Sensitivity Analysis in Bayesian Networks
title_zh: You Only Derive Once (YODO)：贝叶斯网络中高效灵敏度分析的自动微分
abstract: Sensitivity analysis measures the influence of a Bayesian network's parameters on a quantity of interest defined by the network, such as the probability of a variable taking a specific value. In particular, the so-called sensitivity value measures the quantity of interest's partial derivative with respect to the network's conditional probabilities. However, finding such values in large networks with thousands of parameters can become computationally very expensive. We propose to use automatic differentiation combined with exact inference to obtain all sensitivity values in a single pass. Our method first marginalizes the whole network once using e.g. variable elimination and then backpropagates this operation to obtain the gradient with respect to all input parameters. We demonstrate our routines by ranking all parameters by importance on a Bayesian network modeling humanitarian crises and disasters, and then show the method's efficiency by scaling it to huge networks with up to 100'000 parameters. An implementation of the methods using the popular machine learning library PyTorch is freely available.
summary: Sensitivity analysis measures the influence of a Bayesian network's parameters on a quantity of interest defined by the network, such as the probability of a variable taking a specific value. In particular, the so-called sensitivity value measures the quantity of interest's partial derivative with respect to the network's conditional probabilities. However, finding such values in large networks with thousands of parameters can become computationally very expensive. We propose to use automatic differentiation combined with exact inference to obtain all sensitivity values in a single pass. Our method first marginalizes the whole network once using e.g. variable elimination and then backpropagates this operation to obtain the gradient with respect to all input parameters. We demonstrate our routines by ranking all parameters by importance on a Bayesian network modeling humanitarian crises and disasters, and then show the method's efficiency by scaling it to huge networks with up to 100'000 parameters. An implementation of the methods using the popular machine learning library PyTorch is freely available.
abstract_zh: 敏感性分析衡量贝叶斯网络参数对网络定义的感兴趣量的影响，例如变量取特定值的概率。特别是，所谓的敏感度值衡量的是兴趣相对于网络条件概率的偏导数的数量。然而，在具有数千个参数的大型网络中查找此类值的计算成本可能会非常昂贵。我们建议使用自动微分结合精确推理来一次性获得所有敏感度值。我们的方法首先使用例如以下方法将整个网络边缘化：变量消除，然后反向传播该操作以获得关于所有输入参数的梯度。我们通过在建模人道主义危机和灾难的贝叶斯网络上按重要性对所有参数进行排名来展示我们的例程，然后通过将其扩展到具有多达 100,000 个参数的大型网络来展示该方法的效率。使用流行的机器学习库 PyTorch 实现这些方法是免费的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为YODO的方法，利用自动微分和精确推理技术，实现贝叶斯网络中高效灵敏度分析。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在大型贝叶斯网络中计算灵敏度值时计算成本极高，需要更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过一次网络边缘化（如变量消除）结合反向传播，一次性获取所有参数的梯度，结合PyTorch实现自动微分。
AI.result: 在人道主义危机模型中验证方法有效性，并扩展至含10万参数的大型网络，证明其高效性。
AI.conclusion: YODO方法通过自动微分与精确推理结合，为大规模贝叶斯网络的灵敏度分析提供了可扩展的解决方案。

search_text: You Only Derive Once (YODO): Automatic Differentiation for Efficient Sensitivity Analysis in Bayesian Networks You Only Derive Once (YODO)：贝叶斯网络中高效灵敏度分析的自动微分 Sensitivity analysis measures the influence of a Bayesian network's parameters on a quantity of interest defined by the network, such as the probability of a variable taking a specific value. In particular, the so-called sensitivity value measures the quantity of interest's partial derivative with respect to the network's conditional probabilities. However, finding such values in large networks with thousands of parameters can become computationally very expensive. We propose to use automatic differentiation combined with exact inference to obtain all sensitivity values in a single pass. Our method first marginalizes the whole network once using e.g. variable elimination and then backpropagates this operation to obtain the gradient with respect to all input parameters. We demonstrate our routines by ranking all parameters by importance on a Bayesian network modeling humanitarian crises and disasters, and then show the method's efficiency by scaling it to huge networks with up to 100'000 parameters. An implementation of the methods using the popular machine learning library PyTorch is freely available. 敏感性分析衡量贝叶斯网络参数对网络定义的感兴趣量的影响，例如变量取特定值的概率。特别是，所谓的敏感度值衡量的是兴趣相对于网络条件概率的偏导数的数量。然而，在具有数千个参数的大型网络中查找此类值的计算成本可能会非常昂贵。我们建议使用自动微分结合精确推理来一次性获得所有敏感度值。我们的方法首先使用例如以下方法将整个网络边缘化：变量消除，然后反向传播该操作以获得关于所有输入参数的梯度。我们通过在建模人道主义危机和灾难的贝叶斯网络上按重要性对所有参数进行排名来展示我们的例程，然后通过将其扩展到具有多达 100,000 个参数的大型网络来展示该方法的效率。使用流行的机器学习库 PyTorch 实现这些方法是免费的。 本文提出了一种名为YODO的方法，利用自动微分和精确推理技术，实现贝叶斯网络中高效灵敏度分析。 Rafael Ballester-Ripoll,Manuele Leonelli cs.AI

authors_text: Yuanpei Chen,Tianhao Wu,Shengjie Wang,Xidong Feng,Jiechuang Jiang,Stephen Marcus McAleer,Yiran Geng,Hao Dong,Zongqing Lu,Song-Chun Zhu,Yaodong Yang
title: Towards Human-Level Bimanual Dexterous Manipulation with Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 通过强化学习实现人类水平的双手灵巧操作
abstract: Achieving human-level dexterity is an important open problem in robotics. However, tasks of dexterous hand manipulation, even at the baby level, are challenging to solve through reinforcement learning (RL). The difficulty lies in the high degrees of freedom and the required cooperation among heterogeneous agents (e.g., joints of fingers). In this study, we propose the Bimanual Dexterous Hands Benchmark (Bi-DexHands), a simulator that involves two dexterous hands with tens of bimanual manipulation tasks and thousands of target objects. Specifically, tasks in Bi-DexHands are designed to match different levels of human motor skills according to cognitive science literature. We built Bi-DexHands in the Issac Gym; this enables highly efficient RL training, reaching 30,000+ FPS by only one single NVIDIA RTX 3090. We provide a comprehensive benchmark for popular RL algorithms under different settings; this includes Single-agent/Multi-agent RL, Offline RL, Multi-task RL, and Meta RL. Our results show that the PPO type of on-policy algorithms can master simple manipulation tasks that are equivalent up to 48-month human babies (e.g., catching a flying object, opening a bottle), while multi-agent RL can further help to master manipulations that require skilled bimanual cooperation (e.g., lifting a pot, stacking blocks). Despite the success on each single task, when it comes to acquiring multiple manipulation skills, existing RL algorithms fail to work in most of the multi-task and the few-shot learning settings, which calls for more substantial development from the RL community. Our project is open sourced at https://github.com/PKU-MARL/DexterousHands.
summary: Achieving human-level dexterity is an important open problem in robotics. However, tasks of dexterous hand manipulation, even at the baby level, are challenging to solve through reinforcement learning (RL). The difficulty lies in the high degrees of freedom and the required cooperation among heterogeneous agents (e.g., joints of fingers). In this study, we propose the Bimanual Dexterous Hands Benchmark (Bi-DexHands), a simulator that involves two dexterous hands with tens of bimanual manipulation tasks and thousands of target objects. Specifically, tasks in Bi-DexHands are designed to match different levels of human motor skills according to cognitive science literature. We built Bi-DexHands in the Issac Gym; this enables highly efficient RL training, reaching 30,000+ FPS by only one single NVIDIA RTX 3090. We provide a comprehensive benchmark for popular RL algorithms under different settings; this includes Single-agent/Multi-agent RL, Offline RL, Multi-task RL, and Meta RL. Our results show that the PPO type of on-policy algorithms can master simple manipulation tasks that are equivalent up to 48-month human babies (e.g., catching a flying object, opening a bottle), while multi-agent RL can further help to master manipulations that require skilled bimanual cooperation (e.g., lifting a pot, stacking blocks). Despite the success on each single task, when it comes to acquiring multiple manipulation skills, existing RL algorithms fail to work in most of the multi-task and the few-shot learning settings, which calls for more substantial development from the RL community. Our project is open sourced at https://github.com/PKU-MARL/DexterousHands.
abstract_zh: 实现人类水平的灵活性是机器人技术中一个重要的开放性问题。然而，即使在婴儿水平上，灵巧的手部操作任务也很难通过强化学习（RL）来解决。困难在于高度的自由度和异构代理（例如手指的关节）之间所需的合作。在这项研究中，我们提出了双手灵巧手基准（Bi-DexHands），这是一种模拟器，涉及两只灵巧手，执行数十个双手操作任务和数千个目标物体。具体来说，Bi-DexHands 中的任务旨在根据认知科学文献匹配不同水平的人类运动技能。我们在 Issac Gym 建造了 Bi-DexHands；这使得高效的 RL 训练成为可能，仅通过一个 NVIDIA RTX 3090 即可达到 30,000+ FPS。我们为不同设置下的流行 RL 算法提供了全面的基准测试；这包括单智能体/多智能体强化学习、离线强化学习、多任务强化学习和元强化学习。我们的结果表明，PPO 类型的同策略算法可以掌握相当于 48 个月大的人类婴儿的简单操作任务（例如，捕捉飞行物体、打开瓶子），而多智能体 RL 可以进一步帮助掌握需要熟练双手合作的操作（例如，举起锅、堆砌积木）。尽管在每一项任务上都取得了成功，但在获得多种操作技能方面，现有的 RL 算法无法在大多数多任务和少样本学习设置中发挥作用，这需要 RL 社区进行更实质性的发展。我们的项目是开源的：https://github.com/PKU-MARL/DexterousHands。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出一个双臂灵巧手操作基准测试平台Bi-DexHands，通过强化学习实现人类水平的双臂灵巧操作。
AI.motivation: 灵巧手操作任务在机器人领域是重要难题，传统强化学习难以处理高自由度和异构智能体协作问题，需建立标准化基准推动算法发展。
AI.method: 构建包含双臂操作任务和数千目标物体的Isaac Gym模拟器，设计符合认知科学的人类运动技能分级任务，并测试单智能体/多智能体、离线、多任务等RL算法。
AI.result: PPO算法可掌握相当于48个月婴儿的简单操作任务，多智能体RL能完成需要双臂协作的复杂任务，但现有算法在多任务和少样本场景表现不足。
AI.conclusion: 当前RL算法在单一任务表现良好，但需RL社区在多任务和元学习方向进行更深入的研究以实现通用操作能力。

authors_text: Zheng He,Zeke Xie,Quanzhi Zhu,Zengchang Qin
title: Sparse Double Descent: Where Network Pruning Aggravates Overfitting
title_zh: 稀疏双重下降：网络剪枝加剧过度拟合的地方
abstract: People usually believe that network pruning not only reduces the computational cost of deep networks, but also prevents overfitting by decreasing model capacity. However, our work surprisingly discovers that network pruning sometimes even aggravates overfitting. We report an unexpected sparse double descent phenomenon that, as we increase model sparsity via network pruning, test performance first gets worse (due to overfitting), then gets better (due to relieved overfitting), and gets worse at last (due to forgetting useful information). While recent studies focused on the deep double descent with respect to model overparameterization, they failed to recognize that sparsity may also cause double descent. In this paper, we have three main contributions. First, we report the novel sparse double descent phenomenon through extensive experiments. Second, for this phenomenon, we propose a novel learning distance interpretation that the curve of $\ell_{2}$ learning distance of sparse models (from initialized parameters to final parameters) may correlate with the sparse double descent curve well and reflect generalization better than minima flatness. Third, in the context of sparse double descent, a winning ticket in the lottery ticket hypothesis surprisingly may not always win.
summary: People usually believe that network pruning not only reduces the computational cost of deep networks, but also prevents overfitting by decreasing model capacity. However, our work surprisingly discovers that network pruning sometimes even aggravates overfitting. We report an unexpected sparse double descent phenomenon that, as we increase model sparsity via network pruning, test performance first gets worse (due to overfitting), then gets better (due to relieved overfitting), and gets worse at last (due to forgetting useful information). While recent studies focused on the deep double descent with respect to model overparameterization, they failed to recognize that sparsity may also cause double descent. In this paper, we have three main contributions. First, we report the novel sparse double descent phenomenon through extensive experiments. Second, for this phenomenon, we propose a novel learning distance interpretation that the curve of $\ell_{2}$ learning distance of sparse models (from initialized parameters to final parameters) may correlate with the sparse double descent curve well and reflect generalization better than minima flatness. Third, in the context of sparse double descent, a winning ticket in the lottery ticket hypothesis surprisingly may not always win.
abstract_zh: 人们通常认为网络剪枝不仅可以降低深度网络的计算成本，还可以通过降低模型容量来防止过拟合。然而，我们的工作令人惊讶地发现网络剪枝有时甚至会加剧过度拟合。我们报告了一种意想不到的稀疏双下降现象，即当我们通过网络剪枝增加模型稀疏性时，测试性能首先变得更差（由于过度拟合），然后变得更好（由于缓解了过度拟合），最后变得更糟（由于忘记了有用的信息）。虽然最近的研究重点关注模型超参数化的深度双下降，但他们未能认识到稀疏性也可能导致双下降。在本文中，我们有三个主要贡献。首先，我们通过大量实验报告了新颖的稀疏双下降现象。其次，针对这种现象，我们提出了一种新颖的学习距离解释，即稀疏模型的$\ell_{2}$学习距离曲线（从初始化参数到最终参数）可能与稀疏双下降曲线很好地相关，并且比最小平坦度更好地反映泛化能力。第三，在稀疏双血统的背景下，彩票假设中的中奖彩票可能并不总是中奖。

AI.tldr: 本文研究网络剪枝导致过拟合加剧的稀疏双重下降现象，并提出新的解释方法。
AI.motivation: 传统观点认为网络剪枝能降低计算成本并防止过拟合，但作者发现剪枝可能反而加剧过拟合，这一现象未被现有研究充分认识。
AI.method: 通过大量实验观察稀疏双重下降现象，提出基于ℓ₂学习距离的解释方法，并验证彩票假设在该现象下的适用性。
AI.result: 发现剪枝过程中测试性能先变差（过拟合）、再变好（过拟合缓解）、最后变差（遗忘有用信息）的稀疏双重下降曲线，且学习距离曲线比平坦度更反映泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 稀疏性可能导致双重下降，挑战了传统剪枝理论，需重新评估剪枝策略对模型泛化的影响。

search_text: Sparse Double Descent: Where Network Pruning Aggravates Overfitting 稀疏双重下降：网络剪枝加剧过度拟合的地方 People usually believe that network pruning not only reduces the computational cost of deep networks, but also prevents overfitting by decreasing model capacity. However, our work surprisingly discovers that network pruning sometimes even aggravates overfitting. We report an unexpected sparse double descent phenomenon that, as we increase model sparsity via network pruning, test performance first gets worse (due to overfitting), then gets better (due to relieved overfitting), and gets worse at last (due to forgetting useful information). While recent studies focused on the deep double descent with respect to model overparameterization, they failed to recognize that sparsity may also cause double descent. In this paper, we have three main contributions. First, we report the novel sparse double descent phenomenon through extensive experiments. Second, for this phenomenon, we propose a novel learning distance interpretation that the curve of $\ell_{2}$ learning distance of sparse models (from initialized parameters to final parameters) may correlate with the sparse double descent curve well and reflect generalization better than minima flatness. Third, in the context of sparse double descent, a winning ticket in the lottery ticket hypothesis surprisingly may not always win. 人们通常认为网络剪枝不仅可以降低深度网络的计算成本，还可以通过降低模型容量来防止过拟合。然而，我们的工作令人惊讶地发现网络剪枝有时甚至会加剧过度拟合。我们报告了一种意想不到的稀疏双下降现象，即当我们通过网络剪枝增加模型稀疏性时，测试性能首先变得更差（由于过度拟合），然后变得更好（由于缓解了过度拟合），最后变得更糟（由于忘记了有用的信息）。虽然最近的研究重点关注模型超参数化的深度双下降，但他们未能认识到稀疏性也可能导致双下降。在本文中，我们有三个主要贡献。首先，我们通过大量实验报告了新颖的稀疏双下降现象。其次，针对这种现象，我们提出了一种新颖的学习距离解释，即稀疏模型的$\ell_{2}$学习距离曲线（从初始化参数到最终参数）可能与稀疏双下降曲线很好地相关，并且比最小平坦度更好地反映泛化能力。第三，在稀疏双血统的背景下，彩票假设中的中奖彩票可能并不总是中奖。 本文研究网络剪枝导致过拟合加剧的稀疏双重下降现象，并提出新的解释方法。 Zheng He,Zeke Xie,Quanzhi Zhu,Zengchang Qin cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Marzieh Gheisari,Javad Amirian,Teddy Furon,Laurent Amsaleg
title: AggNet: Learning to Aggregate Faces for Group Membership Verification
title_zh: AggNet：学习聚合面孔以进行组成员身份验证
abstract: In some face recognition applications, we are interested to verify whether an individual is a member of a group, without revealing their identity. Some existing methods, propose a mechanism for quantizing precomputed face descriptors into discrete embeddings and aggregating them into one group representation. However, this mechanism is only optimized for a given closed set of individuals and needs to learn the group representations from scratch every time the groups are changed. In this paper, we propose a deep architecture that jointly learns face descriptors and the aggregation mechanism for better end-to-end performances. The system can be applied to new groups with individuals never seen before and the scheme easily manages new memberships or membership endings. We show through experiments on multiple large-scale wild-face datasets, that the proposed method leads to higher verification performance compared to other baselines.
summary: In some face recognition applications, we are interested to verify whether an individual is a member of a group, without revealing their identity. Some existing methods, propose a mechanism for quantizing precomputed face descriptors into discrete embeddings and aggregating them into one group representation. However, this mechanism is only optimized for a given closed set of individuals and needs to learn the group representations from scratch every time the groups are changed. In this paper, we propose a deep architecture that jointly learns face descriptors and the aggregation mechanism for better end-to-end performances. The system can be applied to new groups with individuals never seen before and the scheme easily manages new memberships or membership endings. We show through experiments on multiple large-scale wild-face datasets, that the proposed method leads to higher verification performance compared to other baselines.
abstract_zh: 在一些人脸识别应用中，我们有兴趣验证一个人是否是某个群体的成员，而不透露他们的身份。一些现有的方法提出了一种机制，用于将预先计算的面部描述符量化为离散嵌入并将它们聚合为一组表示。然而，这种机制仅针对给定的封闭个体集进行优化，并且每次更改组时都需要从头开始学习组表示。在本文中，我们提出了一种联合学习面部描述符和聚合机制的深层架构，以实现更好的端到端性能。该系统可以应用于新的团体，其中的个人以前从未见过，并且该方案可以轻松管理新的成员资格或成员资格结束。我们通过对多个大规模野生人脸数据集的实验表明，与其他基线相比，所提出的方法具有更高的验证性能。

AI.tldr: 提出AggNet架构，用于在不暴露个体身份的情况下验证某人是否属于特定群体。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在封闭集合上优化，无法处理动态变化的群体成员，且需重新学习群体表示。
AI.method: 联合学习人脸描述符与聚合机制，支持新群体动态成员管理，端到端优化。
AI.result: 在多个大规模数据集上，AggNet的验证性能优于其他基线方法。
AI.conclusion: AggNet通过动态聚合机制有效解决群体成员验证问题，适用于实际场景。

authors_text: Chaojian Yu,Bo Han,Li Shen,Jun Yu,Chen Gong,Mingming Gong,Tongliang Liu
title: Understanding Robust Overfitting of Adversarial Training and Beyond
title_zh: 了解对抗性训练及其他方面的鲁棒过度拟合
abstract: Robust overfitting widely exists in adversarial training of deep networks. The exact underlying reasons for this are still not completely understood. Here, we explore the causes of robust overfitting by comparing the data distribution of \emph{non-overfit} (weak adversary) and \emph{overfitted} (strong adversary) adversarial training, and observe that the distribution of the adversarial data generated by weak adversary mainly contain small-loss data. However, the adversarial data generated by strong adversary is more diversely distributed on the large-loss data and the small-loss data. Given these observations, we further designed data ablation adversarial training and identify that some small-loss data which are not worthy of the adversary strength cause robust overfitting in the strong adversary mode. To relieve this issue, we propose \emph{minimum loss constrained adversarial training} (MLCAT): in a minibatch, we learn large-loss data as usual, and adopt additional measures to increase the loss of the small-loss data. Technically, MLCAT hinders data fitting when they become easy to learn to prevent robust overfitting; philosophically, MLCAT reflects the spirit of turning waste into treasure and making the best use of each adversarial data; algorithmically, we designed two realizations of MLCAT, and extensive experiments demonstrate that MLCAT can eliminate robust overfitting and further boost adversarial robustness.
summary: Robust overfitting widely exists in adversarial training of deep networks. The exact underlying reasons for this are still not completely understood. Here, we explore the causes of robust overfitting by comparing the data distribution of \emph{non-overfit} (weak adversary) and \emph{overfitted} (strong adversary) adversarial training, and observe that the distribution of the adversarial data generated by weak adversary mainly contain small-loss data. However, the adversarial data generated by strong adversary is more diversely distributed on the large-loss data and the small-loss data. Given these observations, we further designed data ablation adversarial training and identify that some small-loss data which are not worthy of the adversary strength cause robust overfitting in the strong adversary mode. To relieve this issue, we propose \emph{minimum loss constrained adversarial training} (MLCAT): in a minibatch, we learn large-loss data as usual, and adopt additional measures to increase the loss of the small-loss data. Technically, MLCAT hinders data fitting when they become easy to learn to prevent robust overfitting; philosophically, MLCAT reflects the spirit of turning waste into treasure and making the best use of each adversarial data; algorithmically, we designed two realizations of MLCAT, and extensive experiments demonstrate that MLCAT can eliminate robust overfitting and further boost adversarial robustness.
abstract_zh: 鲁棒过拟合广泛存在于深度网络的对抗训练中。其确切的根本原因尚不完全清楚。在这里，我们通过比较\emph{non-overfit}（弱对手）和\emph{overfitted}（强对手）对抗训练的数据分布来探讨鲁棒过拟合的原因，并观察到弱对手生成的对抗数据的分布主要包含小损失数据。然而，强对手产生的对抗数据在大损失数据和小损失数据上的分布更加多样化。鉴于这些观察，我们进一步设计了数据消融对抗训练，并发现一些不值得对抗强度的小损失数据会导致强对抗模式下的鲁棒过拟合。为了缓解这个问题，我们提出\emph{最小损失约束对抗训练}（MLCAT）：在小批量中，我们像往常一样学习大损失数据，并采取额外的措施来增加小损失数据的损失。从技术上讲，当数据变得容易学习时，MLCAT 会阻碍数据拟合，以防止鲁棒的过度拟合；从哲学上来说，MLCAT体现了变废为宝、物尽其用的精神；在算法上，我们设计了 MLCAT 的两种实现，大量的实验表明 MLCAT 可以消除鲁棒的过度拟合，并进一步提高对抗性的鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究对抗训练中鲁棒过拟合现象的成因，并提出一种新的训练方法以缓解该问题。
AI.motivation: 对抗训练中鲁棒过拟合现象普遍存在，但其根本原因尚未完全明确，需探索其成因并提出解决方案。
AI.method: 通过对比弱对抗和强对抗训练的数据分布，设计数据消融实验，提出基于最小损失约束的对抗训练方法（MLCAT），对小损失数据施加额外约束以提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.result: 实验表明MLCAT能有效消除鲁棒过拟合现象，并进一步提升对抗鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: MLCAT通过合理利用对抗样本中的小损失数据，实现了鲁棒过拟合的缓解与对抗性能的提升，体现了资源优化利用的哲学思想。

Given these observations, we further designed data ablation adversarial training and identify that some small-loss data which are not worthy of the adversary strength cause robust overfitting in the strong adversary mode. To relieve this issue, we propose \emph{minimum loss constrained adversarial training} (MLCAT): in a minibatch, we learn large-loss data as usual, and adopt additional measures to increase the loss of the small-loss data. Technically, MLCAT hinders data fitting when they become easy to learn to prevent robust overfitting; philosophically, MLCAT reflects the spirit of turning waste into treasure and making the best use of each adversarial data; algorithmically, we designed two realizations of MLCAT, and extensive experiments demonstrate that MLCAT can eliminate robust overfitting and further boost adversarial robustness. 鲁棒过拟合广泛存在于深度网络的对抗训练中。其确切的根本原因尚不完全清楚。在这里，我们通过比较\emph{non-overfit}（弱对手）和\emph{overfitted}（强对手）对抗训练的数据分布来探讨鲁棒过拟合的原因，并观察到弱对手生成的对抗数据的分布主要包含小损失数据。然而，强对手产生的对抗数据在大损失数据和小损失数据上的分布更加多样化。鉴于这些观察，我们进一步设计了数据消融对抗训练，并发现一些不值得对抗强度的小损失数据会导致强对抗模式下的鲁棒过拟合。为了缓解这个问题，我们提出\emph{最小损失约束对抗训练}（MLCAT）：在小批量中，我们像往常一样学习大损失数据，并采取额外的措施来增加小损失数据的损失。从技术上讲，当数据变得容易学习时，MLCAT 会阻碍数据拟合，以防止鲁棒的过度拟合；从哲学上来说，MLCAT体现了变废为宝、物尽其用的精神；在算法上，我们设计了 MLCAT 的两种实现，大量的实验表明 MLCAT 可以消除鲁棒的过度拟合，并进一步提高对抗性的鲁棒性。 本文研究对抗训练中鲁棒过拟合现象的成因，并提出一种新的训练方法以缓解该问题。 Chaojian Yu,Bo Han,Li Shen,Jun Yu,Chen Gong,Mingming Gong,Tongliang Liu cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Lukas Wolf,Ard Kastrati,Martyna Beata Płomecka,Jie-Ming Li,Dustin Klebe,Alexander Veicht,Roger Wattenhofer,Nicolas Langer
title: A Deep Learning Approach for the Segmentation of Electroencephalography Data in Eye Tracking Applications
title_zh: 眼动追踪应用中脑电图数据分割的深度学习方法
comment: 21 pages, Published at the Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) 2022
comments: 21 pages, Published at the Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) 2022
abstract: The collection of eye gaze information provides a window into many critical aspects of human cognition, health and behaviour. Additionally, many neuroscientific studies complement the behavioural information gained from eye tracking with the high temporal resolution and neurophysiological markers provided by electroencephalography (EEG). One of the essential eye-tracking software processing steps is the segmentation of the continuous data stream into events relevant to eye-tracking applications, such as saccades, fixations, and blinks. Here, we introduce DETRtime, a novel framework for time-series segmentation that creates ocular event detectors that do not require additionally recorded eye-tracking modality and rely solely on EEG data. Our end-to-end deep learning-based framework brings recent advances in Computer Vision to the forefront of the times series segmentation of EEG data. DETRtime achieves state-of-the-art performance in ocular event detection across diverse eye-tracking experiment paradigms. In addition to that, we provide evidence that our model generalizes well in the task of EEG sleep stage segmentation.
summary: The collection of eye gaze information provides a window into many critical aspects of human cognition, health and behaviour. Additionally, many neuroscientific studies complement the behavioural information gained from eye tracking with the high temporal resolution and neurophysiological markers provided by electroencephalography (EEG). One of the essential eye-tracking software processing steps is the segmentation of the continuous data stream into events relevant to eye-tracking applications, such as saccades, fixations, and blinks. Here, we introduce DETRtime, a novel framework for time-series segmentation that creates ocular event detectors that do not require additionally recorded eye-tracking modality and rely solely on EEG data. Our end-to-end deep learning-based framework brings recent advances in Computer Vision to the forefront of the times series segmentation of EEG data. DETRtime achieves state-of-the-art performance in ocular event detection across diverse eye-tracking experiment paradigms. In addition to that, we provide evidence that our model generalizes well in the task of EEG sleep stage segmentation.
abstract_zh: 眼睛注视信息的收集为了解人类认知、健康和行为的许多关键方面提供了一个窗口。此外，许多神经科学研究通过脑电图 (EEG) 提供的高时间分辨率和神经生理学标记来补充从眼球追踪获得的行为信息。眼动追踪软件的基本处理步骤之一是将连续数据流分割成与眼动追踪应用相关的事件，例如扫视、注视和眨眼。在这里，我们介绍 DETRtime，这是一种新颖的时间序列分割框架，它创建不需要额外记录眼动追踪模式并仅依赖于脑电图数据的眼部事件检测器。我们基于端到端深度学习的框架将计算机视觉的最新进展带到了脑电图数据时间序列分割的最前沿。 DETRtime 在不同眼动追踪实验范例中的眼部事件检测方面实现了最先进的性能。除此之外，我们提供的证据表明我们的模型在脑电图睡眠阶段分割任务中具有良好的泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的EEG数据分割方法DETRtime，用于眼动追踪应用中的眼动事件检测。
AI.motivation: 眼动追踪需要高时间分辨率的神经生理数据支持，传统方法依赖多模态数据或复杂预处理，而EEG数据本身包含关键神经标记。
AI.method: 开发DETRtime框架，结合计算机视觉领域的时间序列分割技术，仅使用EEG数据实现眼动事件检测。
AI.result: 在多种眼动实验范式中达到最先进性能，并证明模型在EEG睡眠阶段分割任务中具有良好泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: DETRtime验证了纯EEG数据驱动的深度学习方法在眼动事件检测中的有效性，为神经科学应用提供新工具。

authors_text: Aliaksandra Shysheya,John Bronskill,Massimiliano Patacchiola,Sebastian Nowozin,Richard E Turner
title: FiT: Parameter Efficient Few-shot Transfer Learning for Personalized and Federated Image Classification
title_zh: FiT：用于个性化和联合图像分类的参数高效的小样本迁移学习
comment: ef:The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR 2023)
comments: ef:The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR 2023)
abstract: Modern deep learning systems are increasingly deployed in situations such as personalization and federated learning where it is necessary to support i) learning on small amounts of data, and ii) communication efficient distributed training protocols. In this work, we develop FiLM Transfer (FiT) which fulfills these requirements in the image classification setting by combining ideas from transfer learning (fixed pretrained backbones and fine-tuned FiLM adapter layers) and meta-learning (automatically configured Naive Bayes classifiers and episodic training) to yield parameter efficient models with superior classification accuracy at low-shot. The resulting parameter efficiency is key for enabling few-shot learning, inexpensive model updates for personalization, and communication efficient federated learning. We experiment with FiT on a wide range of downstream datasets and show that it achieves better classification accuracy than the leading Big Transfer (BiT) algorithm at low-shot and achieves state-of-the art accuracy on the challenging VTAB-1k benchmark, with fewer than 1% of the updateable parameters. Finally, we demonstrate the parameter efficiency and superior accuracy of FiT in distributed low-shot applications including model personalization and federated learning where model update size is an important performance metric.
summary: Modern deep learning systems are increasingly deployed in situations such as personalization and federated learning where it is necessary to support i) learning on small amounts of data, and ii) communication efficient distributed training protocols. In this work, we develop FiLM Transfer (FiT) which fulfills these requirements in the image classification setting by combining ideas from transfer learning (fixed pretrained backbones and fine-tuned FiLM adapter layers) and meta-learning (automatically configured Naive Bayes classifiers and episodic training) to yield parameter efficient models with superior classification accuracy at low-shot. The resulting parameter efficiency is key for enabling few-shot learning, inexpensive model updates for personalization, and communication efficient federated learning. We experiment with FiT on a wide range of downstream datasets and show that it achieves better classification accuracy than the leading Big Transfer (BiT) algorithm at low-shot and achieves state-of-the art accuracy on the challenging VTAB-1k benchmark, with fewer than 1% of the updateable parameters. Finally, we demonstrate the parameter efficiency and superior accuracy of FiT in distributed low-shot applications including model personalization and federated learning where model update size is an important performance metric.
abstract_zh: 现代深度学习系统越来越多地部署在个性化和联合学习等情况下，需要支持 i) 少量数据学习，以及 ii) 通信高效的分布式训练协议。在这项工作中，我们开发了 FiLM Transfer (FiT)，它通过结合迁移学习（固定的预训练主干和微调的 FiLM 适配器层）和元学习（自动配置的朴素贝叶斯分类器和情景训练）的思想来满足图像分类设置中的这些要求，以产生在低样本下具有卓越分类精度的参数高效模型。由此产生的参数效率是实现小样本学习、个性化廉价模型更新以及高效通信联邦学习的关键。我们在广泛的下游数据集上进行了 FiT 实验，结果表明，它在低样本情况下比领先的 Big Transfer (BiT) 算法实现了更好的分类精度，并在具有挑战性的 VTAB-1k 基准上实现了最先进的精度，可更新参数少于 1%。最后，我们展示了 FiT 在分布式低样本应用中的参数效率和卓越的准确性，包括模型个性化和联邦学习，其中模型更新大小是一个重要的性能指标。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FiT方法，实现参数高效的少样本迁移学习，应用于个性化和联邦图像分类。
AI.motivation: 现代深度学习需在小样本数据和通信高效的分布式场景（如联邦学习）中运行，现有方法存在参数冗余或准确率不足的问题。
AI.method: 结合迁移学习（预训练骨干+微调FiLM适配器）与元学习（自动配置朴素贝叶斯分类器+情景训练），实现参数高效模型。
AI.result: 在低样本场景下优于BiT算法，在VTAB-1k基准达到SOTA准确率（参数仅为其1%），且在联邦学习等分布式任务中表现优异。
AI.conclusion: FiT通过参数高效设计，在少样本个性化与联邦学习中兼顾模型精度与通信效率，具有重要应用价值。

authors_text: Yuwei Cai,Huanlin Li,Zhun Fan,Juncao Hong,Peng Xu,Hui Cheng,Xiaomi Zhu,Bingliang Hu,Zhifeng Hao
title: VG-Swarm: A Vision-based Gene Regulation Network for UAVs Swarm Behavior Emergence
title_zh: VG-Swarm：基于视觉的无人机群体行为出现基因调控网络
comment: This work has been submitted to the IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L) for possible publication
comments: This work has been submitted to the IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L) for possible publication
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) dynamic encirclement is an emerging field with great potential. Researchers often get inspiration from biological systems, either from macro-world like fish schools or bird flocks etc, or from micro-world like gene regulatory networks (GRN). However, most swarm control algorithms rely on centralized control, global information acquisition, and communications among neighboring agents. In this work, we propose a distributed swarm control method based purely on vision and GRN without any direct communications, in which swarm agents of e.g. UAVs can generate an entrapping pattern to encircle an escaping target of UAV based purely on their installed omnidirectional vision sensors. A finite-state-machine (FSM) describing the behavioral model of each drone is also designed so that a swarm of drones can accomplish searching and entrapping of the target collectively in an integrated way. We verify the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed method in various simulation and real-world experiments.
summary: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) dynamic encirclement is an emerging field with great potential. Researchers often get inspiration from biological systems, either from macro-world like fish schools or bird flocks etc, or from micro-world like gene regulatory networks (GRN). However, most swarm control algorithms rely on centralized control, global information acquisition, and communications among neighboring agents. In this work, we propose a distributed swarm control method based purely on vision and GRN without any direct communications, in which swarm agents of e.g. UAVs can generate an entrapping pattern to encircle an escaping target of UAV based purely on their installed omnidirectional vision sensors. A finite-state-machine (FSM) describing the behavioral model of each drone is also designed so that a swarm of drones can accomplish searching and entrapping of the target collectively in an integrated way. We verify the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed method in various simulation and real-world experiments.
abstract_zh: 无人机动态包围是一个潜力巨大的新兴领域。研究人员经常从生物系统中获得灵感，要么来自鱼群或鸟群等宏观世界，要么来自基因调控网络（GRN）等微观世界。然而，大多数群体控制算法依赖于集中控制、全局信息获取以及相邻代理之间的通信。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种纯粹基于视觉和GRN的分布式群体控制方法，无需任何直接通信，其中群体代理例如无人机可以纯粹基于其安装的全向视觉传感器生成诱捕图案来包围无人机逃跑的目标。还设计了描述每架无人机行为模型的有限状态机（FSM），以便一群无人机能够以集成的方式集体完成对目标的搜索和诱捕。我们在各种仿真和实际实验中验证了所提出方法的有效性和效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于视觉和基因调控网络（GRN）的分布式无人机群控制方法，实现无需直接通信的动态围捕行为。
AI.motivation: 现有无人机群控制算法依赖集中式控制、全局信息获取及邻近通信，存在效率与扩展性局限，需借鉴生物系统实现分布式自主决策。
AI.method: 设计融合视觉感知与GRN的分布式控制框架，结合有限状态机（FSM）建模无人机行为，通过视觉传感器实时感知目标并自主生成围捕模式。
AI.result: 通过仿真与真实实验验证，该方法在多种场景下有效实现目标搜索与围捕，且无需通信即可完成群体协作。
AI.conclusion: 提出的方法为无人机群动态围捕提供了无需通信的分布式解决方案，展示了生物启发机制在群体智能中的应用潜力。
